040924.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ta-li-ne wu-ni-ya-nv-hv-gi a-s-ga-ya di-ge-wi i-yu-li-s-ta-nv-hi; hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-lv-quo-da U-ne-la-nv-hi, a-yv o-tsi-ga-ta-ha na-s-gi hi-a a-s-ga-ya a-s-ga-na ge-sv-i.

040925.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ; ᏑᏓᎴᎩ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ; ᏗᏥᎨᏫ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ.
040925.english: He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
040925.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-ne-tsv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-yu-no a-s-ga-na yi-gi v-tla yi-tsi-ga-ta-ha; su-da-le-gi tsi-ga-ta-ha; di-tsi-ge-wi tsi-ge-sv-gi hna-quo i-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha.

040926.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᏨᏁᎴ ᏕᏣᏍᏚᎢᎡᎴ ᏘᎦᏙᎵ?
040926.english: Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?
040926.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ta-li-ne hi-a nu-ni-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-do tsv-ne-le de-tsa-s-du-i-e-le ti-ga-do-li?

040927.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏳᎳ ᎢᏨᏃᏁᎸ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏣᏛᏓᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏣᏚᎵ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎢᏣᏛᎪᏗᏱ?  ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎪ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏚᎵᎭ ᎡᏥᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
040927.english: He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his disciples?
040927.phonetic: U-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; ga-yu-la i-tsv-no-ne-lv, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsa-dv-da-s-ta-ne-i; ga-do-no i-tsa-du-li ta-li-ne i-tsa-dv-go-di-yi?  Na-s-quo-s-go ni-hi i-tsa-du-li-ha e-tsi-s-da-wa-di-do-hi i-tsa-li-s-do-di-yi.

040928.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎦᎬᏩᏂᏐᏢᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᏴᏍᎩᏂ ᎼᏏ ᎣᏥᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
040928.english: Then they reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses' disciples.
040928.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ga-gv-wa-ni-so-tlv-ta-nv-gi, a-le hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ni-hi na-s-gi hi-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, a-yv-s-gi-ni Mo-si o-tsi-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

040929.cherokee: ᎣᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏬᏁᏔᏅᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᎠ ᎥᏝ ᏲᏥᎦᏔᎭᏧᏓᎴᏅᎢ.
040929.english: We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.
040929.phonetic: O-tsi-ga-ta-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi Mo-si u-wo-ne-ta-nv-i; na-s-gi-ni hi-a v-tla yo-tsi-ga-ta-ha-tsu-da-le-nv-i.

040930.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; Ꮀ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᏂᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏥᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᎢ, ᎠᏃ ᏥᏓᎩᏍᏚᎢᎡᎸ ᏗᏥᎦᏙᎵ.
040930.english: The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.
040930.phonetic: Na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Ho u-s-qua-ni-go-di-yu na-s-gi, ni-tsi-ga-ta-hv-na tsi-gi tsu-da-le-nv-i, a-no tsi-da-gi-s-du-i-e-lv di-tsi-ga-do-li.

040931.cherokee: ᎢᏗᎦᏔᎭᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᏓᏛᎦᏁᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᎢ, ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏛᎦᏁᎰᎢ.
040931.english: Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.
040931.phonetic: I-di-ga-ta-ha-ye-no U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-da-dv-ga-ne-hv-na ge-sv a-ni-s-ga-na-i, i-yu-s-gi-ni gi-lo U-ne-la-nv-hi a-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi yi-gi, a-le na-s-gi a-da-nv-te-s-gv i-ya-dv-ne-hi yi-gi, na-s-gi a-dv-ga-ne-ho-i.

040932.cherokee: ᎡᎶᎯ ᏧᏙᏢᏅ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎨᎦᏛᎦᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏚᏍᏚᎢᎡᎸ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ ᏗᎨᏫ ᎤᏕᏅᎯ.
040932.english: Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.
040932.phonetic: E-lo-hi tsu-do-tlv-nv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv v-tla yi-ge-ga-dv-ga-no gi-lo du-s-du-i-e-lv di-ga-do-li di-ge-wi u-de-nv-hi.

040933.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᏙᎬᏩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏏ.
040933.english: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.
040933.phonetic: I-yu-no hi-a na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya U-ne-la-nv-hi nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi ni-ge-sv-na yi-gi, v-tla ye-li go-hu-s-di yi-do-gv-wa-lv-wi-s-da-si.

040934.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏅᎲᎢ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎨᏒ ᏣᏕᏅᎩ, ᏥᎪᏃ ᎠᏴ ᎢᏍᎩᏰᏲᎲᏍᎦ?  ᎥᎬᏩᏄᎪᏫᏒᎩᏃ.
040934.english: They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
040934.phonetic: U-ni-ne-tsv hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; nv-hv-i a-s-ga-ni ge-sv tsa-de-nv-gi, Tsi-go-no a-yv i-s-gi-ye-yo-hv-s-ga?  V-gv-wa-nu-go-wi-sv-gi-no.

040935.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏛᎦᏅᎩ ᎬᏩᏄᎪᏫᏒᎢ; ᎤᏩᏛᎲᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᏲᎢᏳᎲᏍᎦᏍᎪ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ?
040935.english: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
040935.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-dv-ga-nv-gi gv-wa-nu-go-wi-sv-i; u-wa-dv-hv-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-yo-i-yu-hv-s-ga-s-go U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi?

040936.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏲᎢᏳᏗᏱ?
040936.english: He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
040936.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-go na-s-gi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, na-s-gi tsi-yo-i-yu-di-yi?

040937.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎪᎥᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏥᏣᎵᏃᎮᏗᎭ.
040937.english: And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.
040937.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-go-v-hi, a-le na-s-gi na tsi-tsa-li-no-he-di-ha.

040938.english: And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.

040939.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏗᏬᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎠᎩᏰᎸᎭ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᎩᎷᏥᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎾᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎾ ᎤᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
040939.english: And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
040939.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Di-wo-go-do-di-yi a-gi-ye-lv-ha e-lo-hi a-gi-lu-tsi-lv, na-s-gi na na-ni-go-wa-ti-s-gv-na u-ni-go-wa-dv-di-yi, a-le na-s-gi na a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-gi di-ni-ge-wi i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi.

040940.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎡᏙᎲ ᎠᏁᏙᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏴ ᏦᏥᎨᏫ?
040940.english: And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
040940.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-no e-do-hv a-ne-do-hi na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-nv-gi, a-le hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-go na-s-quo a-yv tso-tsi-ge-wi?

040941.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᏥᎨᏫ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎨᏥᏍᎦᏅᎨᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎣᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ ᎢᏣᏗᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᎾᎯᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎵᏃᎯᏯᏍᎦ.
040941.english: Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.
040941.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-yu-no di-tsi-ge-wi yi-ge-se-i v-tla yi-ge-tsi-s-ga-nv-ge-i, a-se-no o-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha i-tsa-di-ha; na-s-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha i-tsi-s-ga-na-hi-yu ge-sv a-li-no-hi-ya-s-ga.

041001.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏴᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᎤᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏣᏘᏂᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎸᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᎾᏌᎲᏍᎩ.
041001.english: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
041001.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; Na-s-gi na ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-yv-s-di-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na a-wi u-ni-no-de-na u-ni-yv-s-di-yi, u-tsa-ti-ni-quo-s-gi-ni ga-lv-s-di-s-gi, na-s-gi ga-no-s-gi-s-gi a-le a-da-na-sa-hv-s-gi.

041002.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏴᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᏗᎦᏘᏯ.
041002.english: But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
041002.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-yv-s-di-s-gi na-s-gi a-wi u-ni-no-de-na di-ga-ti-ya.

041003.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏍᏚᎢᎡᎰᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏫ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎪ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏙᎥ ᏓᏯᏂᏍᎪ ᎠᏫ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏘᏂᏒᏍᎩᏂ ᏕᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ.
041003.english: To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.
041003.phonetic: Na-s-gi ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-ga-ti-ya u-s-du-i-e-ho-i, a-le a-wi a-na-dv-gi-s-go ka-ne-gv-i, a-le du-na-do-v da-ya-ni-s-go a-wi tsu-tse-li-ga, a-le da-ti-ni-sv-s-gi-ni de-ga-nu-go-wi-s-go-i.

041004.cherokee: ᏕᎦᏄᎪᏩᏃ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᏫ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᏓᎴᏁᎰᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏫ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏕᎪᎢ; ᎠᏃᎵᎪᏰᏃ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ;
041004.english: And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.
041004.phonetic: De-ga-nu-go-wa-no tsu-tse-li-ga a-wi, i-gv-yi da-le-ne-ho-i, a-le a-wi gv-wa-s-da-wa-de-go-i; a-no-li-go-ye-no ka-ne-gv-i;

041005.cherokee: ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎬᏂᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ, ᎠᎾᎵᎡᎰᏉ, ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏯᏃᎵᎪ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎠᏂᏁᎬᎢ.
041005.english: And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.
041005.phonetic: Nv-wa-da-le-s-gi-ni v-tla yv-gv-ni-s-da-wa-du-ga, a-na-li-e-ho-quo, v-tla-ye-no ya-no-li-go nv-wa-na-da-le a-ni-ne-gv-i.

041006.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏃᎵᏤ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅᎢ.
041006.english: This parable spake Jesus unto them: but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them.
041006.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a da-tli-lo-s-dv Tsi-sa du-wo-ne-ta-nv-gi; a-se-no v-tla yu-no-li-tse na-s-gi nu-s-dv du-wo-ne-ta-nv-i.

041007.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ, ᎠᏴ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏫ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
041007.english: Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.
041007.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ta-li-ne Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha, A-yv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-wi u-na-tse-li-ga.

041008.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎯ ᎠᏴ ᎣᏂ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏓᎾᏌᎲᏍᎩ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏫ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎬᏩᎾᏛᎦᏁᎴᎢ.
041008.english: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.
041008.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv i-gv-yi u-ni-lu-tsv-hi a-yv o-ni, na-s-gi a-ni-no-s-gi-s-gi a-le a-na-da-na-sa-hv-s-gi.  A-se-no a-wi v-tla yi-gv-wa-na-dv-ga-ne-le-i.

041009.cherokee: ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏴ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᏴᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏎ  ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏴᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏛᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎦᏄᎸᏒᎢ.
041009.english: I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.
041009.phonetic: Ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-yv; I-yu-no gi-lo a-yv a-gi-yv-s-di-s-ge-s-di, a-se  a-tsi-s-de-lv-di ge-se-s-di, a-le u-yv-s-di a-le u-nu-go-i-s-di ge-se-s-di, a-le u-wa-dv-di ge-se-s-di ga-nu-lv-sv-i.

041010.cherokee: ᎦᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩ ᎦᎷᎪᎢ, ᎤᏃᏍᎩᏍᏗᏱᏉ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᎯᏍᏗᏱᏉ ᎤᏰᎸᏐᎢ:  ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎷᏥᎸ, ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎢᏨᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
041010.english: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.
041010.phonetic: Ga-no-s-gi-s-gi ga-lu-go-i, u-no-s-gi-s-di-yi-quo a-le u-da-hi-s-di-yi-quo u-ye-lv-so-i:  A-yv a-gi-lu-tsi-lv, gv-ni-dv u-na-tse-li-ga i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, a-le u-tsa-ta-nv-hi u-na-tse-li-ga i-tsv-li-s-do-di-yi.

041011.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᏫ-ᏗᏥᎦᏘᏯ.  ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᏫ-ᏗᎦᏘᏯ ᎬᏅᎢ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏓᏁᎰ ᎠᏫ.
041011.english: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
041011.phonetic: A-yv a-qua-da-nv-ti a-wi--di-tsi-ga-ti-ya.  U-da-nv-ti a-wi--di-ga-ti-ya gv-nv-i e-la-di da-ne-ho a-wi.

041012.cherokee: ᎠᏥᏅᏏᏛᏍᎩᏂ, ᎠᏫ-ᏗᎦᏘᏯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏫ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᏩᏯ ᏣᎢᏒᎢ ᏩᎪᏩᏛ, ᏕᎬᏕᎪ ᎠᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏘᏍᎪᎢ; ᏩᏯᏃ ᏕᎦᏂᏱᏍᎪ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏗᎦᎴᏯᏍᎪ ᎠᏫ.
041012.english: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.
041012.phonetic: A-tsi-nv-si-dv-s-gi-ni, a-wi--di-ga-ti-ya ni-ge-sv-na, a-le a-wi tsu-tse-li-ga ni-ge-sv-na, wa-ya tsa-i-sv-i wa-go-wa-dv, de-gv-de-go a-wi a-le a-li-ti-s-go-i; wa-ya-no de-ga-ni-yi-s-go a-le da-di-ga-le-ya-s-go a-wi.

041013.english: The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.

041014.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᏫ-ᏗᏥᎦᏘᏯ; ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎬᎩᎦᏔᎭ,
041014.english: I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine.
041014.phonetic: A-yv a-qua-da-nv-ti a-wi--di-tsi-ga-ti-ya; a-le de-tsi-ga-ta-ha di-qua-tse-li-ga, a-le di-qua-tse-li-ga gv-gi-ga-ta-ha,

041015.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏣᎩᎦᏔᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏥᏥᎦᏔᎭ.  ᎠᎴ ᎬᏅ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏕᏥᏁᎭ ᎠᏫ.
041015.english: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.
041015.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya A-ga-yv-li-ge tsa-gi-ga-ta-ha, a-le a-yv A-ga-yv-li-ge tsi-tsi-ga-ta-ha.  A-le gv-nv e-la-di de-tsi-ne-ha a-wi.

041016.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏄᎾᏓᎴ ᎠᏫ ᏓᎩᎧᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏯᏂᏯᎠ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏎ ᏙᏓᎦᏘᏃᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏛᎾᏛᎦᏂ ᏥᏁᎬᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏅᏛᎾᎵᏍᏔᏂ; ᎠᎴ ᏌᏉ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏫ-ᏗᎦᏘᏯ.
041016.english: And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd.
041016.phonetic: A-le nu-na-da-le a-wi da-gi-ka-ha, na-s-gi v-tla hi-a u-ni-yv-s-di-yi ya-ni-ya-a; na-s-gi na-s-quo a-se do-da-ga-ti-no-li, a-le dv-na-dv-ga-ni tsi-ne-gv-i; a-le su-na-da-tlu-gi nv-dv-na-li-s-ta-ni; a-le sa-quo ge-se-s-di a-wi--di-ga-ti-ya.

041017.cherokee: ᎡᎳᏗ ᏥᏥᎥᏍᎦ ᎬᏅᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎠᎩᎩᏏᏐᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎠᎩᎨᏳᎭ.
041017.english: Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.
041017.phonetic: E-la-di tsi-tsi-v-s-ga gv-nv-i na-s-gi ta-li-ne a-gi-gi-si-so-di tsi-gi na-s-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha A-ga-yv-li-ge a-gi-ge-yu-ha.

041018.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏴᏛᎩᎩᎡᎵ, ᎠᏋᏒᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏖᏛ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏓᏥᏂ.  ᎠᎩᎭ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎬᎩᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᎭ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎬᎩᎩᏏᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎠᎩᏁᏤᎸ.
041018.english: No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.
041018.phonetic: V-tla gi-lo yv-dv-gi-gi-e-li, a-quv-sv-quo-s-gi-ni a-qua-da-nv-te-dv e-la-di da-tsi-ni.  A-gi-ha ye-li-quo e-la-di gv-gi-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-gi-ha ye-li-quo gv-gi-gi-si-so-di ge-sv-i.  Na-s-gi hi-a nu-s-di E-do-da a-gi-ne-tse-lv.

041019.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏔᎵ ᏄᎾᏓᏛᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒᎢ.
041019.english: There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.
041019.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ta-li-ne ta-li nu-na-da-dv-gi A-ni-tsu-si nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi na-s-gi nu-we-sv-i.

041020.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏴᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏯᎠ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎸᏃᏘᎭ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᎡᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎭ?
041020.english: And many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad; why hear ye him?
041020.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-dv-gi-no u-na-da-su-yv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; A-s-gi-na u-ya-a, a-le u-lv-no-ti-ha; ga-do-no i-e-tsa-dv-da-s-da-ne-ha?

041021.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪ ᏥᎧᏁᎦ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏯᎢ ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏓᏍᏚᎢᏏ ᏗᎨᏫ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ.
041021.english: Others said, These are not the words of him that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of the blind?
041021.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Na-s-gi hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we tsi-ka-ne-ga v-tla a-s-gi-na u-ya-i u-ne-i-s-di yi-gi; tsi-go a-s-gi-na ye-li yi-da-s-du-i-si di-ge-wi di-ga-do-li.

041022.cherokee: ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᎤᏂᏅᎦᎸᎲ ᏓᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎳ ᎨᏒᎩ.
041022.english: And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.
041022.phonetic: Di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi-no u-ni-nv-ga-lv-hv da-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, a-le go-la ge-sv-gi.

041023.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎡᏙᎲᎩ ᎤᏛᏅ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏐᎵᎹᏅ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᏲᏓᏝᎲᎢ.
041023.english: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon's porch.
041023.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no e-do-hv-gi u-dv-nv--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, So-li-ma-nv u-tse-li-ga a-yo-da-tla-hv-i.

041024.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎬᏩᏓᏡᏫᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎳᎪ ᏅᏓᎪᎯᏥ ᏍᎩᏰᎵᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ?  ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏍᎩᏃᎲᏏ ᏚᏳᎪᏛᎢ.
041024.english: Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
041024.phonetic: Hna-quo-no A-ni-tsu-si gv-wa-da-tlu-wi-s-ta-nv-gi, hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-la-go nv-da-go-hi-tsi s-gi-ye-li-i-s-di-s-ge-s-di?  I-yu-no ni-hi ga-lo-ne-dv ge-se-s-di, s-gi-no-hv-si du-yu-go-dv-i.

041025.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏚᏂᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏨᏃᏁᎸᎩ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏍᎩᏲᎢᏳᏁᎢ.  ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏥᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ ᎡᏙᏓ ᏚᏙᏍᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏋᏁᎭ.
041025.english: Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father's name, they bear witness of me.
041025.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv-gi hi-a du-ni-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsv-no-ne-lv-gi, a-se-no v-tla yi-s-gi-yo-i-yu-ne-i.  Di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di tsi-da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha E-do-da du-do-s-dv-i, na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv na-quv-ne-ha.

041026.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏦᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᏂᎯ ᎠᏫ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏥᏂᏨᏪᏎᎸᎩ.
041026.english: But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.
041026.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni v-tla yi-tso-hi-yu-hv-s-ga, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha ni-hi a-wi di-qua-tse-li-ga ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, na-s-gi-ya tsi-ni-tsv-we-se-lv-gi.

041027.cherokee: ᎠᏫ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎪ ᏥᏁᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎦᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏍᏓᏩᏕᎪᎢ,
041027.english: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:
041027.phonetic: A-wi di-qua-tse-li-ga a-na-dv-gi-s-go tsi-ne-gv-i, a-le a-yv ga-tsi-ga-ta-ha, a-le gv-ni-s-da-wa-de-go-i,

041028.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎦᏥᏁᎰᎢ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏗᏆᏑᎦᎸᏙᏗ ᏱᎩ.
041028.english: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.
041028.phonetic: A-le a-li-s-qua-di-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na gv-ni-dv ga-tsi-ne-ho-i, v-tla-no i-lv-hi-yu tsu-ni-yo-hu-hi-s-di yi-gi, a-le v-tla gi-lo di-qua-su-ga-lv-do-di yi-gi.

041029.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎡᏙᏓ ᏗᎩᎧᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᏟ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎾᏂᎥᎢ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎩᎶ ᎡᏙᏓ ᏗᎬᏩᏑᎦᎸᏙᏗ ᏱᎩ.
041029.english: My Father, which gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father's hand.
041029.phonetic: Na-s-gi na E-do-da di-gi-ka-ne-lv-hi u-tli u-li-ni-gi-di-yu E-s-ga-quo na-ni-v-i, v-tla-no ye-li gi-lo E-do-da di-gv-wa-su-ga-lv-do-di yi-gi.

041030.english: I and my Father are one.

041031.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏅᏯ ᏚᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏍᏙᏗ.
041031.english: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.
041031.phonetic: Hna-quo-no A-ni-tsu-si ta-li-ne nv-ya du-ni-gi-sv-gi di-gv-wa-ni-s-do-di.

041032.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏚᏂᏪᏎᎸᎩ, ᎤᏣᏔ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏙᏙᏱ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏨᏴᏁᎸ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎠᎩᏍᏛᏗᎠ ᏅᏯ ᏥᏕᏍᎩᏴᏂᏍᏗᎭ?
041032.english: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
041032.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv-gi hi-a du-ni-we-se-lv-gi, U-tsa-ta o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv e-do-do-yi nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi gv-ni-ge-sv ni-tsv-yv-ne-lv; Ga-do u-s-di na-s-gi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di a-gi-s-dv-di-a nv-ya tsi-de-s-gi-yv-ni-s-di-ha?

041033.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏂᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎨᏣᏍᏛᏓ ᏅᏯ ᏱᏙᎨᏨᏴᏂᏍᏓ, ᎯᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᏍᎩᏂ, ᏂᎯ ᏴᏫᏉ ᏥᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏣᏤᎸᏍᎦ.
041033.english: The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.
041033.phonetic: A-ni-tsu-si u-ni-ne-tsv hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv v-tla yv-ge-tsa-s-dv-da nv-ya yi-do-ge-tsv-yv-ni-s-da, hi-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-gv-s-gi-ni, ni-hi yv-wi-quo tsi-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi tsa-tse-lv-s-ga.

041034.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᏚᏁᏤᎸᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏂ ᎬᏅ ᏱᎪᏪᎳ ᏗᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ; ᏂᎯ ᎢᏨᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᏆᏛᏅᎩ?
041034.english: Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?
041034.phonetic: Tsi-sa du-ne-tse-lv-gi; tla-s-go hi-a yi-ni gv-nv yi-go-we-la di-tsi-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-yi; ni-hi i-tsv-ne-la-nv-hi, a-qua-dv-nv-gi?

041035.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎢᏣᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏬᏎᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎨᏥᏁᏤᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎤᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ,
041035.english: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;
041035.phonetic: I-yu-no i-tsa-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-wo-se-lv-hi yi-gi, ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga ge-tsi-ne-tse-lv-hi, a-le go-we-li u-yo-i-s-di ni-ge-sv-na yi-gi,

041036.cherokee: ᏥᎪ ᎯᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᎭ ᎢᎡᏦᏎᎭ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᎤᎸᏉᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏅᏏᏛ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ "ᎠᏴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ" ᏥᎦᏛ?
041036.english: Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?
041036.phonetic: Tsi-go hi-so-tlv-i-s-di-ha i-e-tso-se-ha A-ga-yv-li-ge-i u-lv-quo-ta-nv-hi a-le e-lo-hi u-lu-hi-s-di-yi u-nv-si-dv, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha "A-yv U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi" tsi-ga-dv?

041037.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏂᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏍᎩᏲᎢᏳᏅᎩ
041037.english: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.
041037.phonetic: I-yu-no E-do-da u-tse-li di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv ni-da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-na i-ge-se-s-di, tle-s-di s-gi-yo-i-yu-nv-gi

041038.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏱᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏴ ᏂᏍᎩᏲᎢᏳᎲᏍᎬᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᏎ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎨᏣᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏦᎯᏳᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎩᏯᎥᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏥᏯᎥᎢ
041038.english: But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.
041038.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni na-s-gi u-tse-li yi-da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha, na-s-quo a-yv ni-s-gi-yo-i-yu-hv-s-gv-na yi-gi, a-se i-tso-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di na-s-gi na di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i; na-s-gi-no ye-li ge-tsa-do-le-ho-hi-s-di a-le ge-tso-hi-yu-di yi-gi A-ga-yv-li-ge-i, a-gi-ya-v-i a-le na-s-gi a-yv tsi-ya-v-i

041039.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᎾᏁᎶᏔᏅᎩ ᎬᏩᏂᏴᏗᏱ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏚᏗᏫᏎᎸᎩ,
041039.english: Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
041039.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ta-li-ne u-na-ne-lo-ta-nv-gi gv-wa-ni-yv-di-yi; a-se-no du-di-wi-se-lv-gi,

041040.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏦᏓᏂ ᏍᎪᎾ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ, ᎾᎿᎭᏣᏂ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᏚᏓᏬᎥᎢ.  ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎤᏪᏙᎸᎩ.
041040.english: And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.
041040.phonetic: A-le Tso-da-ni s-go-na wu-lo-sv-gi, na-hna Tsa-ni i-gv-yi-yi du-da-wo-v-i.  Na-hna-no u-we-do-lv-gi.

041041.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏛᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᏂ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏱᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏣᏂ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᏃᎮᎸ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎨᏎᎢ.
041041.english: And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.
041041.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-dv-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi, a-le hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Tsa-ni v-tla u-s-qua-ni-go-di yi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-i, a-se-no ni-ga-v Tsa-ni nu-we-sv u-no-he-lv hi-a na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya ge-se-i.

041042.english: And many believed on him there.

041101.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏢᎬᎩ, ᎳᏏᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏇᏗᏂᏱ ᎡᎯ, ᎺᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎸᎢ ᎹᏗ ᎤᏂᏚᎲᎢ.
041101.english: Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
041101.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya u-tlv-gv-gi, La-si-la tsu-do-i-dv, Que-di-ni-yi e-hi, Me-li a-le u-lv-i Ma-di u-ni-du-hv-i.

041102.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎺᎵ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ ᏧᎶᏁᏔᏁ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏘᏰᎬ ᏧᏩᏔᏁ ᏥᏚᏅᎦᎸᎡᎴ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏙ ᎳᏏᎳ ᎤᏢᎬᎩ.
041102.english: (It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
041102.phonetic: Na-s-gi Me-li a-tlo-ne-di tsu-lo-ne-ta-ne U-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le u-s-ti-ye-gv tsu-wa-ta-ne tsi-du-nv-ga-lv-e-le tsu-la-si-de-ni, na-s-gi nv-do La-si-la u-tlv-gv-gi.

041103.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏧᏙ ᏓᏳᎾᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᎤᏛᎪᏗ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᏪᏒᎩ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎯᎨᏳᎢ ᏥᎩ ᎤᏢᎦ.
041103.english: Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
041103.phonetic: Hna-quo-no tsu-do da-yu-na-da-nv-sv-gi u-dv-go-di, hi-a nv-da-yu-ni-we-sv-gi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, gv-ni-yu-quo hi-ge-yu-i tsi-gi u-tlv-ga.

041104.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏢᎦ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏙᏗᏱᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
041104.english: When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.
041104.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-dv-ga-nv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Hi-a na-s-gi tsu-tlv-ga-v-tla u-yo-hu-hi-s-di yi-gi, a-tsi-lv-quo-do-di-yi-quo-s-gi-ni U-ne-la-nv-hi, na-s-gi-no U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

041105.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏧᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎹᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎸᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎳᏏᎳ.
041105.english: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
041105.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no tsu-ge-yu-hi-yu-ge-sv-gi Ma-di a-le u-lv-i a-le La-si-la.

041106.cherokee: ᎤᏛᎦᏅᏃ ᎤᏢᎬᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᎡᏙᎲᎢ ᎠᏏ ᏔᎵ ᏅᎤᏙᏓᏋᎩ.
041106.english: When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
041106.phonetic: U-dv-ga-nv-no u-tlv-gv-i, na-hna e-do-hv-i a-si ta-li nv-u-do-da-quv-gi.

041107.cherokee: ᎣᏂᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᏔᎵᏁ ᏧᏗᏱ ᏫᏗᎶᎯ.
041107.english: Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.
041107.phonetic: O-ni-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; Ta-li-ne Tsu-di-yi wi-di-lo-hi.

041108.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎪᎯᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏁᎶᏔᏅᎩ ᏅᏯ ᏗᎨᏨᏂᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᏔᎵᏁᏍᎪᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᏮᏛᎯᎶᏏ?
041108.english: His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
041108.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, v-tla yi-go-hi-gi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-ne-lo-ta-nv-gi nv-ya di-ge-tsv-ni-s-do-di-yi, ta-li-ne-s-go-no na-hna wv-dv-hi-lo-si?

041109.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᏱᎩ ᏏᎦ?  ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᎦ ᏤᏙᎰᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏬᏕᎯᎰᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏚᎸᏌᏛᎢ.
041109.english: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
041109.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv-gi; Tla-s-go ta-la-du i-yu-tli-lo-dv yi-gi si-ga?  I-yu-no gi-lo i-ga tse-do-ho-i, v-tla yi-du-wo-de-hi-ho-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-go a-go-wa-ti-s-gv i-ga a-ni e-lo-hi du-lv-sa-dv-i.

041110.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᏃᏱ ᏤᏙᎰᎢ, ᏚᏬᏕᎯᎰᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪ ᎢᎦ ᏂᏚᎸᏌᏓᏕᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
041110.english: But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
041110.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni e-no-yi tse-do-ho-i, du-wo-de-hi-ho-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-go i-ga ni-du-lv-sa-da-de-hv-na ge-sv-i.

041111.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎳᏏᎳ ᎢᎦᎵᎢ ᎦᎵᎭ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏓᎨᏏ, ᏮᏓᏥᏰᏍᏔᏂ.
041111.english: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.
041111.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; hna-quo-no hi-a nu-du-we-se-lv-gi; La-si-la i-ga-li-i ga-li-ha; a-se-no da-ge-si, wv-da-tsi-ye-s-ta-ni.

041112.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏱᎦᎵᎭ ᏓᏳᏗᏩᏏᏉ.
041112.english: Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
041112.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-yu-no yi-ga-li-ha da-yu-di-wa-si-quo.

041113.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᎦᏛᎬᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒ ᏥᏌ:  ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎠᎦᎵᎲᏉ ᏦᏯᏪᏐᎵᎰ ᎦᏛᎦ ᎤᏁᎵᏒᎩ.
041113.english: Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
041113.phonetic: A-se-no u-yo-hu-sv ga-dv-gv-gi na-s-gi nu-we-sv Tsi-sa:  na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na a-ga-li-hv-quo tso-ya-we-so-li-ho ga-dv-ga u-ne-li-sv-gi.

041114.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎳᏏᎳ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ;
041114.english: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
041114.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-ni-ge-sv Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; La-si-la u-yo-hu-sv;

041115.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᎩᏰᎸᎭ ᎾᎿᎭᏫᏂᎨᏙᎲᎾ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ, ᏂᎯ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ, ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏗᏱ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎡᏗᏩᏛᎱᎦ.
041115.english: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
041115.phonetic: A-le o-si-yu a-gi-ye-lv-ha na-hna wi-ni-ge-do-hv-na tsi-ge-sv-gi, ni-hi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha, i-tso-hi-yu-di-yi.  A-se-no e-di-wa-dv-hu-ga.

041116.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏓᎻ, ᏗᏗᎹ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎢᏕᎾ, ᎠᏲᎱᏒᎭ ᎢᎦᏠᏯᏍᏔᏅᎭ.
041116.english: Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.
041116.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Da-mi, Di-di-ma tsu-do-i-dv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi u-na-li-go-hi a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, Na-s-quo a-yv i-de-na, a-yo-hu-sv-ha i-ga-tlo-ya-s-ta-nv-ha.

041117.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎤᏙᎴᎱᏒᎩ ᎦᏳᎳ ᏅᎩ ᏧᏙᏓᏉᏛ ᎬᏩᏂᏅᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ.
041117.english: Then when Jesus came, he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.
041117.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa u-lu-tsv u-do-le-hu-sv-gi ga-yu-la nv-gi tsu-do-da-quo-dv gv-wa-ni-nv-nv-hi ge-sv a-tse-li-s-dv-i.

041118.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏇᏗᏂᏃ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎾᎥ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᏔᎵᎭᏉ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ.
041118.english: Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
041118.phonetic: Na-s-gi Que-di-ni-no ga-du-hv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi na-v ge-sv-gi, ta-li-ha-quo i-yu-tli-lo-dv.

041119.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎬᏩᏂᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎹᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎺᎵ ᎬᏩᏂᏄᏬᎯᏍᏔᏂᎸᎩ ᎤᏂᏙ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
041119.english: And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
041119.phonetic: A-le u-ni-tsa-dv A-ni-tsu-si gv-wa-ni-lu-tse-lv-gi Ma-di a-le Me-li gv-wa-ni-nu-wo-hi-s-ta-ni-lv-gi u-ni-do u-yo-hu-sv i-yu-s-di.

041120.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎹᏗ ᎤᏛᎦᏅᏉ ᏥᏌ ᏣᎢᏒᎢ ᏫᏚᏠᏒᎩ.  ᎺᎵᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎵᏦᏕᏉ ᎬᏬᎸᎩ.
041120.english: Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat still in the house.
041120.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Ma-di u-dv-ga-nv-quo Tsi-sa tsa-i-sv-i wi-du-tlo-sv-gi.  Me-li-s-gi-ni ga-li-tso-de-quo gv-wo-lv-gi.

041121.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎹᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏥᏌ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏂ ᏱᎮᏙᎮᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎬᏩᏲᎱᏎ ᎥᎩᏙ.
041121.english: Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
041121.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Ma-di a-le nu-we-se-lv-gi Tsi-sa; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-yu-no a-ni yi-he-do-he-i, v-tla yi-gv-wa-yo-hu-se v-gi-do.

041122.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᏔᏲᏎᎸ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᏣᏁᏗᏉ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
041122.english: But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee.
041122.phonetic: A-se-no tsi-ga-ta-ha na-s-quo go-hi ge-sv ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di yi-ta-yo-se-lv U-ne-la-nv-hi, tsa-ne-di-quo ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi.

041123.english: Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.

041124.cherokee: ᎹᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏙᏗᎴᎯᏌᏂᏒ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
041124.english: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.
041124.phonetic: Ma-di hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-ga-ta-ha do-di-le-hi-sa-ni-sv di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv u-li-s-qua-lv-di i-ga ge-se-s-di.

041125.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏴ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏛ.  ᎩᎶ ᎠᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎢᏳ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎬᏁᏍᏗ.
041125.english: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
041125.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; A-yv di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv a-le gv-ni-dv.  Gi-lo a-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di, i-yu na-s-quo u-yo-hu-sv-hi yi-gi, gv-ne-s-di.

041126.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎬᏁᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ?
041126.english: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?
041126.phonetic: Gi-lo-no gv-ne-s-di a-le a-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di, v-tla i-lv-hi-yu u-yo-hu-hi-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di.  Ho-hi-yu-hv-s-ga-s-go na-s-gi hi-a?

041127.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᎥ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᎪᎢᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᏂᎯ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎯ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ.
041127.english: She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
041127.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; V-v, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi; go-i-yu-hv-s-ga ni-hi Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-hi, U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi, na-s-gi e-lo-hi u-lu-hi-s-di tsi-ge-sv-gi.

041128.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏭᏯᏅᎲᎩ ᎤᎸᎢ ᎺᎵ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏗᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎦᎷᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏣᏯᏂᎭ.
041128.english: And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.
041128.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv u-da-nv-sv-gi, a-le u-de-li-dv wu-ya-nv-hv-gi u-lv-i Me-li, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Di-de-yo-hv-s-gi ga-lu-gi a-le di-tsa-ya-ni-ha.

041129.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᏱᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎴᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᎷᏤᎸᎩ.
041129.english: As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.
041129.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-dv-ga-nv yi-la-quo i-yv-dv du-le-nv-gi, a-le wu-lu-tse-lv-gi.

041130.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏏ ᏱᎦᎷᎨ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎹᏗ ᏫᏚᏠᏒ ᎡᏙᎲᎩ.
041130.english: Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.
041130.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no v-tla a-si yi-ga-lu-ge ga-du-hv-i, na-hna-quo-s-gi-ni Ma-di wi-du-tlo-sv e-do-hv-gi.

041131.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᎤᏪᎸ ᎠᏂᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏄᏬᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᎺᎵ ᎤᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᏚᎴᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᎪᏨ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏗᎦᏂᏌᎲ ᏩᎦᏘ ᎾᎿᎭᏮᏛᏍᎪᏂᎵ.
041131.english: The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.
041131.phonetic: Hna-quo-no A-ni-tsu-si ga-li-tso-de u-we-lv a-ni-ni, a-le gv-wa-nu-wo-hi-s-di-s-gi, u-ni-go-hv Me-li u-li-s-di-yu du-le-nv a-le u-nu-go-tsv gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Di-ga-ni-sa-hv wa-ga-ti na-hna wv-dv-s-go-ni-li.

041132.cherokee: ᎺᎵᏃ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏥᏌ ᎡᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲ, ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏂ ᏱᎮᏙᎮᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎥᎩᏙ ᏱᎬᏩᏲᎱᏎᎢ.
041132.english: Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
041132.phonetic: Me-li-no u-lu-tsv Tsi-sa e-do-hv-i, a-le u-go-hv, du-la-s-gv e-la-di u-da-nv-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-yu-no a-ni yi-he-do-he-i, v-tla v-gi-do yi-gv-wa-yo-hu-se-i.

041133.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᏓᏠᏱᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎪᎲ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏍᏓᏩᏙᎸᎯ ᏓᎾᏠᏱᎲᎢ, ᎡᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏓᏛᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ.
041133.english: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,
041133.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-go-hv da-tlo-yi-hv-i, a-le du-go-hv A-ni-tsu-si na-s-gi u-ni-s-da-wa-do-lv-hi da-na-tlo-yi-hv-i, e-hi-s-di u-da-nv-da-dv-gi, a-le u-de-ya-do-di nu-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi.

041134.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎭᏢ ᎡᏥᏅᏁᎢ?  ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎡᎭᎦᏔ, ᎬᏬᏎᎸᎩ.
041134.english: And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
041134.phonetic: A-le hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ha-tlv e-tsi-nv-ne-i?  Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, e-ha-ga-ta, gv-wo-se-lv-gi.

041136.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏂᎦᎡ ᎤᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ.
041136.english: Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!
041136.phonetic: Hna-quo-no A-ni-tsu-si hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Gv-ni-yu-quo ni-ga-e u-ge-yu-hi-yu ge-se-i.

041137.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏴᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎯᎠ ᏗᎨᏫ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ ᏧᏍᏚᎢᎡᎸᎯ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏂᎬᏩᏂᏌᏁ ᏄᏲᎱᏒᎾ ᏱᎦᎨᏎ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ?
041137.english: And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?
041137.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no u-na-da-su-yv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Tla-s-go hi-a di-ge-wi di-ga-do-li tsu-s-du-i-e-lv-hi, na-s-quo na-s-gi yi-ni-gv-wa-ni-sa-ne nu-yo-hu-sv-na yi-ga-ge-se hi-a a-s-ga-ya?

041138.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎡᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏓᏛ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ.  ᎤᏍᏓᎦᎸ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏯ ᎠᏍᏚᏛᎩ.
041138.english: Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave. It was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.
041138.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no ta-li-ne e-hi-s-di u-da-nv-da-dv a-tse-li-s-dv u-lu-tsv-gi.  U-s-da-ga-lv ge-sv-gi, a-le nv-ya a-s-du-dv-gi.

041139.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᏅᏯ ᎢᏥᎲᎾ.  ᎹᏗ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎤᏙ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎪᎯ ᎠᏎ ᎠᏒᎦ, ᏅᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᏒᎭ ᎦᏰᏥᏂᏌᏅᎯ.
041139.english: Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days.
041139.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-sv-gi, Nv-ya i-tsi-hv-na.  Ma-di u-yo-hu-sv-hi u-do, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, go-hi a-se a-sv-ga, nv-gi-ye-no nu-sv-ha ga-ye-tsi-ni-sa-nv-hi.

041140.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏂᎬᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏣᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
041140.english: Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?
041140.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tla-s-go hi-a yi-ni-gv-we-se-le-i; i-yu-no ho-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di, tsa-go-wa-dv-di ge-se-s-di ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv u-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

041141.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏅᏯ ᎤᏂᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎦᏅᎢ.  ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᏌᎳᏓᏅᎩ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎡᏙᏓ, ᎬᏯᎵᎡᎵᏤᎭ ᏥᏍᏆᏛᎦᏁᎸ.
041141.english: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.
041141.phonetic: Hna-quo-no nv-ya u-ni-hv-s-gi u-yo-hu-sv-hi ga-nv-i.  Tsi-sa-no du-sa-la-da-nv-gi di-ga-do-li, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; E-do-da, gv-ya-li-e-li-tse-ha tsi-s-qua-dv-ga-ne-lv.

041142.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎢ ᏍᏆᏛᎦᏁᎲᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂ ᏣᏂᏙᎾᎠ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏂᏥᏫ, ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᏂᎯ ᏅᏓᏍᎩᏅᏏᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
041142.english: And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me.
041142.phonetic: A-le tsi-ga-ta-ha ni-go-hi-lv-i s-qua-dv-ga-ne-hv-i; a-se-no yv-wi a-ni tsa-ni-do-na-a nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da na-s-gi tsi-ni-tsi-wi, u-no-hi-yu-di-yi ni-hi nv-da-s-gi-nv-si-dv ge-sv-i.

041143.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎳᏏᎳ, ᎡᎯᏄᎪᎢ.
041143.english: And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.
041143.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, a-s-da-ya u-we-lu-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; La-si-la, e-hi-nu-go-i.

041144.cherokee: ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯᏃ ᏓᏳᏄᎪᏨᎩ, ᏧᏬᏰᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏚᏓᎸᏍᏛᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏓᎸᏛ ᏗᏄᏬ, ᎤᎧᏛᏃ ᎠᏯᏠ ᎤᏓᎸᏍᏛᎩ.  ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎡᏣᎸᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏤᏥᏲᎯ.
041144.english: And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.
041144.phonetic: U-yo-hu-sv-hi-no da-yu-nu-go-tsv-gi, tsu-wo-ye-ni a-le tsu-la-si-de-ni du-da-lv-s-dv-gi di-ga-lv-da-lv-dv di-nu-wo, u-ka-dv-no a-ya-tlo u-da-lv-s-dv-gi.  Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; E-tsa-lv-sv a-le tse-tsi-yo-hi.

041145.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏴᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎺᎵ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎢ, ᎬᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ.
041145.english: Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.
041145.phonetic: A-le u-ni-tsa-dv-gi u-na-da-su-yv-gi A-ni-tsu-si Me-li gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-hi, a-le u-ni-go-hv-hi na-s-gi Tsi-sa nu-dv-ne-lv-i, gv-wo-hi-yu-nv-gi.

041146.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᏗᏂᏅ ᏭᏂᎶᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏚᏂᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎩ.
041146.english: But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
041146.phonetic: I-ga-dv-s-gi-ni A-ni-qua-li-si di-ni-nv wu-ni-lo-sv-gi, a-le wi-du-ni-no-ne-lv-gi Tsi-sa nu-dv-ne-lv-gi.

041147.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏉᎵᏏ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏛᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏓᏛᏁᎭ?  ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏣᏔ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ.
041147.english: Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
041147.phonetic: Hna-quo-no nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-le A-ni-quo-li-si du-ni-la-wi-dv-gi, a-le hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ga-do i-da-dv-ne-ha?  Hi-a-ye-no a-s-ga-ya u-tsa-ta u-s-qua-ni-go-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha.

041148.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏰᏕᎵᏎᎭ, ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏓᎬᏬᎯᏳᏂ, ᎠᏂᎶᎻᏃ ᏛᏂᎷᏥ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎨᎩᎩᎡᎵ ᎢᎦᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ ᏙᏓᎨᎩᏯᏅᎡᎵ.
041148.english: If we let him thus alone, all men will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.
041148.phonetic: I-yu-no na-s-gi u-ne-la-gi ye-de-li-se-ha, ni-ga-di-yu da-gv-wo-hi-yu-ni, A-ni-lo-mi-no dv-ni-lu-tsi a-le da-ge-gi-gi-e-li i-ga-tse-li-go-hi a-le di-ga-tse-li yv-wi do-da-ge-gi-ya-nv-e-li.

041149.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏴᏫᏃ ᎤᏓᏑᏴᎩ, ᎧᏱᏆ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᏕᏘᏴᏌᏗᏒ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ,
041149.english: And one of them, named Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,
041149.phonetic: A-si-yv-wi-no u-da-su-yv-gi, Ka-yi-qua tsu-do-i-dv, na-hi-yu u-de-ti-yv-sa-di-sv nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi ge-sv-gi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; V-tla go-hu-s-di yi-tsi-ga-ta-ha,

041150.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏣᏓᏅᏖᎭ ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᎨᎦᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎩᎶ ᏱᏓᏲᎱᎯᏎᎭ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏯᎾᏗᏒᎲᏍᎦ.
041150.english: Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.
041150.phonetic: A-le v-tla yi-tsa-da-nv-te-ha u-tli o-si-yu i-ge-ga-li-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i, a-si-yv-wi gi-lo yi-da-yo-hu-hi-se-ha yv-wi, a-no u-ni-tsa-ti u-nv-sv ya-na-di-sv-hv-s-ga.

041151.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᏛ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ; ᎾᎯᏳ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏕᏘᏴᏌᏗᏒ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏧᏲᎱᎯᏎᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏑᎾᏓᎴᎩ ᎨᏒ ᏴᏫ,
041151.english: And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;
041151.phonetic: Na-s-gi nu-we-sv-gi, v-tla u-wa-sv u-da-nv-te-dv yi-ge-se-i; na-hi-yu a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi-s-gi-ni ge-sv i-yu-s-di u-de-ti-yv-sa-di-sv u-do-le-ho-sv-gi Tsi-sa tsu-yo-hu-hi-se-di-yi na-s-gi su-na-da-le-gi ge-sv yv-wi,

041152.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏑᎾᏓᎴᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏅᏒ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏌᏉ ᎢᏧᏩᏁᏗᏱ ᏧᏪᏟᏐᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏗᎦᎴᏲᏨᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏪᏥ.
041152.english: And not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.
041152.phonetic: A-le v-tla na-s-gi na su-na-da-le-gi ge-sv u-nv-sv, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni sa-quo i-tsu-wa-ne-di-yi tsu-we-tli-so-di-yi u-na-di-ga-le-yo-tsv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-we-tsi.

041153.english: Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.

041154.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏥᏌ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏱᏚᏪᎳᏗᏙᎴ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ; ᎾᎿᎭᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ, ᎢᎾᎨ ᎾᎥ ᏭᎶᏅᎩ, ᎢᏆᎻ ᏚᏙᎥ ᏗᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏪᏅᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
041154.english: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
041154.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di Tsi-sa hna-quo v-tla i-lv-hi-yu gv-ni-ge-sv yi-du-we-la-di-do-le A-ni-tsu-si; na-hna-s-gi-ni u-ni-gi-sv-gi, i-na-ge na-v wu-lo-nv-gi, I-qua-mi du-do-v di-ga-du-hv-i, a-le na-hna u-we-nv-gi a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

041155.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏧᏏᏃ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎯᏗᏒᎩ; ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎾᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏭᏂᎶᏒᎩ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎾᏍᏆᎵᏍᎬᎾ ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ, ᎤᎾᏓᏅᎦᎸᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ.
041155.english: And the Jews' passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.
041155.phonetic: A-ni-tsu-si-no u-na-tse-li-ga ka-no-hi-ye-gi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi u-s-qua-lv-hi-di-sv-gi; u-ni-tsa-dv-gi-no na-hna u-na-ni-gi-sv-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wu-ni-lo-sv-gi a-si-quo na-s-qua-li-s-gv-na ka-no-hi-ye-gi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi, u-na-da-nv-ga-lv-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi.

041156.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏂᏲᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏛᏅᏗᎦᎳᏫᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎥ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏤᎵᎭ?  ᏝᏍᎪ ᏓᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬ ᏴᏓᎦᎷᏥ?
041156.english: Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
041156.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ni-yo-lv-gi, a-le u-dv-nv-di-ga-la-wi-s-di-yi a-ni-do-na-v hi-a ni-du-na-da-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-do i-tse-li-ha?  Tla-s-go da-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv yv-da-ga-lu-tsi?

041157.cherokee: ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏯᎦᏔᎭ ᎡᏙᎲᎢ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏩᏁᏗᏱ, ᎤᏂᏂᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ.
041157.english: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.
041157.phonetic: Nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv-no a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-le na-s-quo A-ni-qua-li-si u-ni-ne-tsv-hi ge-sv-gi, i-yu-no gi-lo ya-ga-ta-ha e-do-hv-i, gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-wa-ne-di-yi, u-ni-ni-yv-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi.

041201.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏑᏓᎵ ᎢᎦ ᏚᏃᏒᎩ ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᏗᏱ ᏥᏌ ᏇᏗᏂ ᏭᎷᏨᎩ, ᎾᎿᎭᎡᎲ ᎳᏏᎳ, ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏥᏚᎴᏔᏅᎩ.
041201.english: Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
041201.phonetic: Hna-quo-no su-da-li i-ga du-no-sv-gi ka-no-hi-ye-gi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi u-s-qua-lv-di-yi Tsi-sa Que-di-ni wu-lu-tsv-gi, na-hna e-hv La-si-la, u-yo-hu-sv-hi tsi-ge-sv-gi, na-s-gi u-yo-hu-sv tsi-du-le-ta-nv-gi.

041202.cherokee: ᎾᎿ ᎭᎬᏩᏍᏓᏴᏅᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎹᏗ ᏚᏕᎳᏍᏔᏅᎩ; ᎳᏏᎳᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᎸᎩ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎢ.
041202.english: There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
041202.phonetic: Na-hna gv-wa-s-da-yv-nv-gi; a-le Ma-di du-de-la-s-ta-nv-gi; La-si-la-s-gi-ni ge-lv-gi a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-i.

041203.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎺᎵ ᎤᎩᏒᎩ ᏑᏓᎨᏛ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ ᎾᏓ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏑᏴᎾ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏅᎵᏰᎥᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏘᏰᎬ ᎤᏩᏔᏅᎩ ᏚᏅᎦᎸᎲᎩ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ.  ᎠᎴ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎩ ᎦᏩᏒᎬ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ.
041203.english: Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.
041203.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Me-li u-gi-sv-gi su-da-ge-dv a-tlo-ne-di na-da tsu-do-i-dv go-hu-s-di na-su-yv-na, u-tsa-ti tsu-gv-wa-lo-di, a-le du-nv-li-ye-v-gi Tsi-sa tsu-la-si-de-ni, a-le u-s-ti-ye-gv u-wa-ta-nv-gi du-nv-ga-lv-hv-gi tsu-la-si-de-ni.  A-le ga-li-tso-de u-ka-li-tsv-gi ga-wa-sv-gv a-tlo-ne-di.

041204.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏓᏏ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏌᏩᏂ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏡᏗᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ;
041204.english: Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, which should betray him,
041204.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-si-yv-wi Tsi-sa gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ge-sv Tsu-da-si I-s-ga-la-di tsu-do-i-dv, Sa-wa-ni u-we-tsi, na-s-gi u-tlu-di-s-gi i-yu-li-s-do-di, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi;

041205.cherokee: ᎦᏙᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏂᎾᏗᏅᏎ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢᏧᏈ ᎠᎩᏏ ᏧᎾᏩᎶᏗ ᏱᏚᏂᏩᏛᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏚᏂᏁᎴᎢ?
041205.english: Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?
041205.phonetic: Ga-do-no hi-a a-tlo-ne-di v-tla yu-ni-na-di-nv-se a-le tso-i-tsu-qui a-gi-si tsu-na-wa-lo-di yi-du-ni-wa-dv-he-i, a-le v-tla u-yo i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi na-s-gi yi-du-ni-ne-le-i?

041206.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏱᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁᎢ, ᎦᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎶᏗ ᎦᏁᎲᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᏃᏍᎩᏍᎬᎢ.
041206.english: This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.
041206.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi, v-tla u-yo i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi o-s-dv i-yu-na-li-s-da-ne-di-yi u-du-li-s-gv yi-nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-ne-i, ga-no-s-gi-s-gi-s-gi-ni ge-sv nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi, a-le de-ga-lo-di ga-ne-hv-i a-le na-hna ga-la-nv-hi ga-no-s-gi-s-gv-i.

041207.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏁᎳᎩ; ᎥᎩᏂᏐᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎦ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ.
041207.english: Then said Jesus, Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.
041207.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; U-ne-la-gi; v-gi-ni-so-di-yi ge-sv i-ga gv-di-s-gi u-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv na-s-gi hi-a.

041208.cherokee: ᎤᏲᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎨᏤᎳᏗᏙᎭ; ᎠᏴᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᏱᏨᏰᎳᏗᏙᎭ.
041208.english: For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
041208.phonetic: U-yo-ye-no i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi ni-go-hi-lv ge-tse-la-di-do-ha; a-yv-s-gi-ni v-tla ni-go-hi-lv yi-tsv-ye-la-di-do-ha.

041209.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎡᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ ᎥᏝ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎳᏏᎳ ᎤᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏧᎴᏔᏅᎯ.
041209.english: Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus' sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
041209.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-dv-gi-no A-ni-tsu-si u-na-dv-ga-nv-hi ge-sv-gi na-hna e-do-hv-i, a-le u-ni-lu-tsv-gi v-tla Tsi-sa u-wa-sv ge-sv yi-nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-ne-i, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni La-si-la u-ni-go-wa-dv-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi, na-s-gi u-yo-hu-sv tsu-le-ta-nv-hi.

041210.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎩ ᎳᏏᎳ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ,
041210.english: But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;
041210.phonetic: A-se-no nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi u-ni-no-he-lv-gi La-si-la na-s-quo u-ni-hi-s-di-yi,

041211.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᏩᏂᏌᏅᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᏫᎬᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ.
041211.english: Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
041211.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no nu-wa-ni-sa-nv-gi u-ni-tsa-dv-gi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-da-nv-sv-gi, a-le Tsi-sa wi-gv-wo-hi-yu-nv-gi.

041212.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᏴᏫ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎯ, ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏥᎷᏥᎵᎻ ᏗᎦᎷᏥᏒᎢ,
041212.english: On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
041212.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv u-ni-tsa-dv-gi yv-wi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi u-ni-lu-tsv-hi, u-na-dv-ga-nv Tsi-lu-tsi-li-mi di-ga-lu-tsi-sv-i,

041213.cherokee: ᏚᏂᏴᎲᎩ ᏧᏪᏲᏔ ᏧᎦᏄᏓᏅᎯᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨᎩ ᏚᎾᏠᏒᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ, ᎰᏌᎾ!  ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏫᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ ᏱᎰᏩ ᏚᏙᏍᏛ ᏨᏓᏯᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
041213.english: Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed is the King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.
041213.phonetic: Du-ni-yv-hv-gi tsu-we-yo-ta tsu-ga-nu-da-nv-hi-dv a-le u-ni-nu-go-tsv-gi du-na-tlo-sv-sv-gi, a-le hi-a nu-ni-we-sv u-ne-lu-nv-gi, Ho-sa-na!  A-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu wi-ni-ga-li-s-da Yi-ho-wa du-do-s-dv tsv-da-ya-i, U-gv-wi-yu-hi I-si-li u-na-tse-li-ga.

041214.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏩᏛᎲ ᎠᎩᎾ ᏐᏈᎵ-ᏗᎦᎵᎠᏅᎯᏛ, ᎤᎩᎸᏅᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ;
041214.english: And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,
041214.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-wa-dv-hv a-gi-na so-qui-li--di-ga-li-a-nv-hi-dv, u-gi-lv-nv-gi, na-s-gi-ya hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la;

041215.cherokee: "ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ ᏌᏯᏂ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᏓᏯᎢ, ᎠᎩᎾ ᏐᏈᎵ-ᏗᎦᎵᎠᏅᎯᏛ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎩᎸᏗ."
041215.english: Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass's colt.
041215.phonetic: "Tle-s-di yi-s-ga-i-he-s-di ni-hi Sa-ya-ni u-we-tsi, gv-ni-yu-quo U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsa-tse-li-ga da-ya-i, a-gi-na so-qui-li--di-ga-li-a-nv-hi-dv u-we-tsi u-gi-lv-di."

041216.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏃᎵᏤ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ, ᏥᏌᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏔᏅ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎾᏅᏓᏛᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏅ ᎪᏪᎸ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎬᏩᏁᎸᎢ.
041216.english: These things understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and that they had done these things unto him.
041216.phonetic: A-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi v-tla na-s-gi tsa-no-li-tse i-gv-yi-yi, Tsi-sa-s-gi-ni a-tsi-lv-quo-ta-nv hna-quo u-na-nv-da-dv-gi na-s-gi hi-a ni-gv-nv go-we-lv a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i, a-le na-s-gi ni-gv-wa-ne-lv-i.

041217.cherokee: ᎢᏧᎳᎭᏃ ᎤᏁᏙᎸᎯ ᏴᏫ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᏭᏯᏅᎲ ᎳᏏᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏅ, ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎩ.
041217.english: The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.
041217.phonetic: I-tsu-la-ha-no u-ne-do-lv-hi yv-wi na-hi-yu a-tse-li-s-dv wu-ya-nv-hv La-si-la, a-le u-yo-hu-sv de-u-le-ta-nv, u-ni-no-he-lv-gi.

041218.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏴᏫ ᏕᎬᏩᏠᏒᎩ, ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎩᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
041218.english: For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.
041218.phonetic: Na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi na-s-quo yv-wi de-gv-wa-tlo-sv-gi, u-na-dv-ga-nv-gi-ye-no na-s-gi u-s-qua-ni-go-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i.

041219.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏣᏙᎴᎰᏍᎦᏍᎪ ᏂᏣᎵᏰᎢᎴᎬᎾ ᏥᎩ?  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏕᏅ.
041219.english: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after him.
041219.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di A-ni-qua-li-si hi-a ni-du-na-da-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsa-do-le-ho-s-ga-s-go ni-tsa-li-ye-i-le-gv-na tsi-gi?  Gv-ni-yu-quo e-lo-hi u-s-da-wa-de-nv.

041220.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎠᏁᎸᎩ ᎤᎾᎵᏙᎵᏍᏔᏂᎸᎯ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ.
041220.english: And there were certain Greeks among them that came up to worship at the feast:
041220.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di a-ni-go-i a-ne-lv-gi u-na-li-do-li-s-ta-ni-lv-hi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi.

041221.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᏈᎵᎩ, ᏇᏣᏱᏗ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ, ᎣᎦᏚᎵᎭ ᎣᏥᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᏥᏌ.
041221.english: The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.
041221.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi Qui-li-gi, Que-tsa-yi-di ga-du-hv Ge-li-li e-hi, a-le gv-wa-ta-yo-se-lv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi, o-ga-du-li-ha o-tsi-go-wa-dv-di-yi Tsi-sa.

041222.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏂᏗ ᏭᏃᏁᎸᎩ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎡᏂᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏈᎵᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏭᏂᏃᏁᎸᎩ.
041222.english: Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.
041222.phonetic: Qui-li-gi u-lu-tsv-gi a-le E-ni-di wu-no-ne-lv-gi; hna-quo-no E-ni-di a-le Qui-li-gi Tsi-sa wu-ni-no-ne-lv-gi.

041223.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎲ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏙᏗᏱ.
041223.english: And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.
041223.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-ne-tse-lv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Hna-quo u-s-qua-lv-hv yv-wi U-we-tsi a-tsi-lv-quo-do-di-yi.

041224.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏌᏉ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎤᎦᏔ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏄᏬᏨᎾ ᎠᎴ ᏄᎪᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᎨᏐ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎢ; ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎪᎯ ᎤᏣᏙ ᎧᏁᏉᎪᎢ.
041224.english: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.
041224.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; I-yu-no sa-quo u-tsa-le-s-di u-ga-ta e-la-di nu-wo-tsv-na a-le nu-go-sv-na yi-gi, u-wa-sv-quo ge-so ni-go-hi-lv-i; i-yu-s-gi-ni u-go-hi u-tsa-do ka-ne-quo-go-i.

041225.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎤᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎬᏅᎢ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᎨᏍᏗ ᎬᏅᎢ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎬᏂᏛ ᏫᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᏗᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ.
041225.english: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
041225.phonetic: Gi-lo u-ge-yu-se-s-di gv-nv-i u-yo-hu-se-di ge-se-s-di; gi-lo-no a-s-ga-ge-s-di gv-nv-i a-ni e-lo-hi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi u-s-qua-ni-go-do-di ge-se-s-di gv-ni-dv wi-na-s-dv-na di-ge-sv gv-di-s-gi.

041226.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᎨᏍᏗ; ᎨᎥᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎡᎮᏍᏗ.  ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏙᏓ ᎤᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
041226.english: If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour.
041226.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo tsi-nv-si-da-s-di ni-ga-li-s-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi a-gi-s-da-wa-de-ge-s-di; ge-v-no na-hna na-s-quo tsi-nv-si-da-s-di e-he-s-di.  I-yu-no gi-lo tsi-nv-si-da-s-di ni-ga-li-s-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi E-do-da u-lv-quo-do-di ge-se-s-di.

041227.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏕᏯᏔᏁᎭ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏓᎦᏛᏂ?  [ᎯᎠᏍᎪ ᏅᏓᏥᏪᏏ,] ᎡᏙᏓ, ᏍᏊᏓᎳᎩ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏣᎩᏍᏆᎸᎡ ᎪᎯ ᎠᎩᏍᏆᎸᎡᏗᏱ ᎠᎩᎷᏥᎸ.
041227.english: Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.
041227.phonetic: Hna-quo a-qua-da-nv-do u-de-ya-ta-ne-ha; ga-do-no da-ga-dv-ni?  [Hi-a-s-go nv-da-tsi-we-si,] E-do-da, s-quu-da-la-gi go-hi ge-sv-i?  A-se-no na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tsa-gi-s-qua-lv-e go-hi a-gi-s-qua-lv-e-di-yi a-gi-lu-tsi-lv.

041228.cherokee: ᎡᏙᏓ, ᎯᎸᏉᏓ ᏕᏣᏙᎥᎢ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏓᏳᏁᏨᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᏳᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏳᎳ ᎠᎩᎸᏉᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏛᏥᎸᏉᏔᏂ.
041228.english: Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a voice from heaven, saying, I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.
041228.phonetic: E-do-da, hi-lv-quo-da de-tsa-do-v-i.  Hna-quo-no ga-lv-la-di da-yu-ne-tsv-gi, hi-a nv-da-yu-we-sv-gi; ga-yu-la a-gi-lv-quo-ta-nv, a-le ta-li-ne dv-tsi-lv-quo-ta-ni.

041229.cherokee: ᏴᏫᏃ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎠᏴᏓᏆᎶᏣ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.  ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏓᏳᏬᏁᏓ.
041229.english: The people therefore, that stood by, and heard it, said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to him.
041229.phonetic: Yv-wi-no na-v a-ni-do-na-i u-na-dv-ga-nv, a-yv-da-qua-lo-tsa, u-na-dv-nv-gi.  I-ga-dv-no hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi da-yu-wo-ne-da.

041230.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᏴ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ, ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ, ᎯᎠ ᎧᏁᎬ ᏥᎦᎾᏄᎪᏥᎦ.
041230.english: Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.
041230.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; V-tla a-yv ge-sv yi-nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da, ni-hi-s-gi-ni ge-sv nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da, hi-a ka-ne-gv tsi-ga-na-nu-go-tsi-ga.

041231.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉ ᎯᎠ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏙᏓᏰᎫᎪᏓᏁᎵ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᏓᏰᏥᏄᎪᏫᏏ.
041231.english: Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.
041231.phonetic: Hna-quo hi-a e-lo-hi do-da-ye-gu-go-da-ne-li, hna-quo u-gv-wi-yu-hi hi-a e-lo-hi u-tse-li-ga da-ye-tsi-nu-go-wi-si.

041232.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎥᎩᏌᎳᏓᏅᎭ, ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᏙᏓᎦᏎᏒᎯ.
041232.english: And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me.
041232.phonetic: A-se-no i-yu-no e-lo-hi v-gi-sa-la-da-nv-ha, na-ni-v yv-wi do-da-ga-se-sv-hi.

041233.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏛᎬᎩ.
041233.english: This he said, signifying what death he should die.
041233.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi, na-s-gi i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di-yi u-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi ga-dv-gv-gi.

041234.cherokee: ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ, ᎠᏴ ᎣᎦᏛᎦᏅ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎡᎲᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏎ ᏓᏰᏥᏌᎳᏓᏂ ᎢᎭᏗᎭ?  ᎦᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ?
041234.english: The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?
041234.phonetic: Yv-wi u-ni-ne-tsv hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi, A-yv o-ga-dv-ga-nv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-yi na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na e-hv-i; Ga-do-no yv-wi U-we-tsi a-se da-ye-tsi-sa-la-da-ni i-ha-di-ha?  Ga-go na-s-gi na yv-wi U-we-tsi?

041235.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏏᏞᎦ ᎢᎦ ᏚᎸᏌᏛ ᎢᏤᏙᎲᎢ; ᎢᏤᏙᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎢᎦ ᏕᏥᎸᏌᏓᏕᎲᎢ; ᎤᎵᏏᎩᎾᏏ ᏱᏧᏢᏓ; ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᎵᏏᎬ ᏤᏙᎰᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏯᎦᏔᎰ ᏩᎦᏛᎢ.
041235.english: Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.
041235.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; A-si-tle-ga i-ga du-lv-sa-dv i-tse-do-hv-i; i-tse-do-he-s-di ni-go-hi-lv i-ga de-tsi-lv-sa-da-de-hv-i; u-li-si-gi-na-si yi-tsu-tlv-da; gi-lo-ye-no u-li-si-gv tse-do-ho-i, v-tla ya-ga-ta-ho wa-ga-dv-i.

041236.cherokee: ᎠᏏ ᎢᎦ ᏥᏕᏥᎸᏌᏓᏕᎭ, ᎢᏦᎯᏳᎲᎦ ᎢᎦ ᏚᎸᏌᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏚᎸᏌᏛ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ ᏥᏌ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᏚᏗᏍᎦᎳᏁᎸᎩ.
041236.english: While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
041236.phonetic: A-si i-ga tsi-de-tsi-lv-sa-da-de-ha, i-tso-hi-yu-hv-ga i-ga du-lv-sa-dv-i, na-s-gi-no ni-hi i-ga du-lv-sa-dv tsu-we-tsi ge-se-s-di.  Na-s-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi Tsi-sa, a-le u-da-nv-sv-gi du-di-s-ga-la-ne-lv-gi.

041237.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏗᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎬᏬᎯᏳᏁᎢ.
041237.english: But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:
041237.phonetic: U-tsa-di-s-gi-ni-no-nv u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-hi ge-sv-gi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, a-se-no v-tla yi-gv-wo-hi-yu-ne-i.

041238.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏅᎩ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎢᏌᏯ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; "ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᎪ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏅ ᎣᎩᏃᎮᎸᎢ?  ᎠᎴ ᎦᎪ ᎠᏥᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎧᏃᎨᏂ?"
041238.english: That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?
041238.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-do-hi-yu-nv-gi u-ne-tsv I-sa-ya a-do-le-ho-s-gi, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; "Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ga-go u-wo-hi-yu-nv o-gi-no-he-lv-i?  A-le ga-go a-tsi-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv U-gv-wi-yu-hi ka-no-ge-ni?"

041239.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏏᏗ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏃᎯᏳᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ; ᏔᎵᏁᏰᏃ ᎢᏌᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ.
041239.english: Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,
041239.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-si-di v-tla ye-li gv-wa-no-hi-yu-di yi-ge-se-i; ta-li-ne-ye-no I-sa-ya hi-a nu-we-se-i.

041240.cherokee: "ᏚᏍᏚᏁᎸ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᏓᏱᏕᎸ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ; ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎪᏩᏛᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏃᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᏂᎾᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏓᏁᏟᏴᎡᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᎦᏥᏅᏬᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ."
041240.english: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; that they should not see with their eyes, nor understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.
041240.phonetic: "Du-s-du-ne-lv di-ni-ga-do-li, a-le du-s-da-yi-de-lv tsu-ni-na-wi; di-gv-wa-ni-go-wa-dv-do-di ni-ge-sv-na di-ni-ga-do-li, a-le gv-wa-no-li-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na u-ni-na-wi, a-le na-s-gi di-gv-wa-na-da-ne-tli-yv-e-di ni-ge-sv-na, a-le di-ga-ga-tsi-nv-wo-di ni-ge-sv-na."

041241.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏎ ᎢᏌᏯ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᏧᎪᎮ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏃᎮᎴᎢ.
041241.english: These things said Esaias, when he saw his glory, and spake of him.
041241.phonetic: Na-s-gi nu-we-se I-sa-ya, na-hi-yu tsu-go-he ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv u-tse-li-ga, a-le na-s-gi tsu-no-he-le-i.

041242.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏱᏄᏅᏁᎴᎢ, ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏱᏙᎩᏄᎪᏩ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎬᎩ.
041242.english: Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
041242.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-dv-gi-s-gi-ni-no-nv na-s-quo u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi gv-wo-hi-yu-nv-gi; a-se-no A-ni-qua-li-si nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi v-tla gv-ni-ge-sv yi-nu-nv-ne-le-i, di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi yi-do-gi-nu-go-wa a-ne-li-s-gv-gi.

041243.cherokee: ᎤᏟᏰᏃ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏓᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
041243.english: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
041243.phonetic: U-tli-ye-no u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi yv-wi u-na-da-lv-quo-do-di ge-sv e-s-ga-quo U-ne-la-nv-hi u-da-lv-quo-do-di ge-sv-i.

041244.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᎩᎶ ᎠᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏴ ᏯᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ, ᏅᏛᎩᏅᏏᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎪᎢ.
041244.english: Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
041244.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-we-lu-nv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi, Gi-lo a-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-gi v-tla a-yv ya-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-ga, nv-dv-gi-nv-si-dv-s-gi-ni go-hi-yu-hv-s-go-i.

041245.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎪᏩᏘᏍᎩ ᏅᏛᎩᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ.
041245.english: And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
041245.phonetic: A-le gi-lo a-yv a-gi-go-wa-ti-s-gi nv-dv-gi-nv-si-dv a-go-wa-ti-s-go-i.

041246.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᎢᎦᎦᏘ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᎩᎷᏥᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎵᏏᎬ ᎤᏕᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
041246.english: I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
041246.phonetic: A-yv i-ga-ga-ti e-lo-hi a-gi-lu-tsi-lv, na-s-gi gi-lo a-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di v-tla hna-quo u-li-si-gv u-de-di yi-ge-se-s-di.

041247.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏛᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏥᏁᎬᎢ, ᏂᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᎾᏃ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᏴ ᏱᏗᏥᏳᎪᏓᏁᎭ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏱᏗᏥᏳᎪᏓᏁᎵᎸ, ᏥᏍᏕᎸᎯᎸᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ.
041247.english: And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
041247.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo a-dv-gi-s-ge-s-di tsi-ne-gv-i, ni-go-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-na-no i-ge-se-s-di, v-tla a-yv yi-di-tsi-yu-go-da-ne-ha; v-tla-ye-no e-lo-hi yi-di-tsi-yu-go-da-ne-li-lv, tsi-s-de-lv-hi-lv-s-gi-ni e-lo-hi.

041248.cherokee: ᎠᎩᏐᏅᎢᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏂᎸᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᏁᎬᎢ ᏧᏬᎪᏓᏁᎯ ᎤᏪᎭ.  ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᎩᏁᏨᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏬᎪᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
041248.english: He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.
041248.phonetic: A-gi-so-nv-i-s-di-s-gi a-le di-da-ni-lv-gi ni-ge-sv-na tsi-ne-gv-i tsu-wo-go-da-ne-hi u-we-ha.  Ka-no-he-dv a-gi-ne-tsv-hi na-s-gi tsu-wo-go-da-ne-di ge-se-s-di u-li-s-qua-lv-di i-ga ge-se-s-di.

041249.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎠᏋᏒᏉ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏖᏛ ᎠᎩᏁᏨᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢᏍᎩᏂ ᏅᏛᎩᏅᏏᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎩᏁᏤᎸ ᎢᏯᎩᏪᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎩᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
041249.english: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
041249.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no a-quv-sv-quo a-qua-da-nv-te-dv a-gi-ne-tsv-hi yi-gi, A-ga-yv-li-ge-i-s-gi-ni nv-dv-gi-nv-si-dv, na-s-gi a-gi-ne-tse-lv i-ya-gi-we-s-di-yi a-gi-wo-ni-hi-s-di-yi.

041250.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ ᏫᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏥᏁᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏥᏪᎠ.
041250.english: And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.
041250.phonetic: A-le tsi-ga-ta-ha na-s-gi u-ne-tsv-hi wi-na-s-dv-na gv-ni-dv ge-sv-i.  Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ni-ga-v tsi-ne-gv-i, na-s-gi-ya A-ga-yv-li-ge-i na-gi-we-se-lv-i, na-s-gi ni-tsi-we-a.

041301.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏃ ᎤᏃᏒᎩ ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ, ᏥᏌ ᎠᎦᏔᎯᏳ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎡᎸ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᏍᏛᏱ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᏧᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎬᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᏧᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᎨᏒᎩ.
041301.english: Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
041301.phonetic: A-si-no u-no-sv-gi ka-no-hi-ye-gi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi, Tsi-sa a-ga-ta-hi-yu ge-sv-gi u-s-qua-lv-e-lv a-ni e-lo-hi u-da-nv-s-di-yi, A-ga-yv-li-ge-s-dv-yi wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi, tsu-ge-yu-hi-yu i-yu-li-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-gi tsu-tse-li-ga e-lo-hi a-ne-hi, gv-li-s-qua-di-s-gi tsu-ge-yu-hi-yu ge-sv-gi.

041302.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎩᎾᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎳᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ ᏧᏓᏏ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎶᏄᎮᏗᏱ, ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏃᏅᏃ,
041302.english: And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, to betray him;
041302.phonetic: a-s-gi-na-no hna-quo u-la-ne-lv-hi ge-sv-gi u-na-wi-yi Tsu-da-si I-s-ga-la-di Sa-wa-ni u-we-tsi na-s-gi u-lo-nu-he-di-yi, u-na-li-s-da-yv-no-nv-no,

041303.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎠᎦᏔᎯᏳ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏕᎤᏲᎯᏎᎸᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏱ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏱ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ,
041303.english: Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;
041303.phonetic: Tsi-sa a-ga-ta-hi-yu ge-sv-gi A-ga-yv-li-ge-i ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di de-u-yo-hi-se-lv-i; a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi-yi nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi ge-sv-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi-yi wu-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv-i,

041304.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎢ ᏕᎤᎴᏅᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏒᎩ ᎠᏄᏬ-ᏗᏑᎴᏗ, ᎤᏓᏠᏍᏔᏅᎩ;
041304.english: He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
041304.phonetic: U-li-s-da-yv-nv-i de-u-le-nv-gi a-le u-nu-we-sv-gi, a-le u-ne-sv-gi a-nu-wo--di-su-le-di, u-da-tlo-s-ta-nv-gi;

041305.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᎠᎹ ᎤᏟᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᏚᏬᏑᎴᎸ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏧᎾᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏅᎦᎸᎡᎸᎩ ᎠᏄᏬ-ᏗᏑᎴᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᏠᏍᏛ ᎤᏩᏔᏅᎩ.
041305.english: After that he poureth water into a bason, and began to wash the disciples' feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
041305.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-te-li-do a-ma u-tli-s-ta-nv-gi, a-le u-le-nv-hv-gi du-wo-su-le-lv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi tsu-na-la-si-de-ni, a-le du-nv-ga-lv-e-lv-gi a-nu-wo--di-su-le-di na-s-gi u-da-tlo-s-dv u-wa-ta-nv-gi.

041306.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏬᎸ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ.  ᏈᏓᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏥᎪ ᏂᎯ ᏙᏓᏍᏉᏑᎴᎵ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂ?
041306.english: Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
041306.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Sa-wa-ni Qui-da u-wo-lv u-lu-tsv-gi.  Qui-da-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tsi-go ni-hi do-da-s-quo-su-le-li di-qua-la-si-de-ni?

041307.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏛᏁᎭ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏱᎦᏔᎭ, ᎣᏂᏍᎩᏂ ᏘᎦᏙᎥᏏ.
041307.english: Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
041307.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-a tsi-ni-ga-dv-ne-ha v-tla go-hi ge-sv yi-ga-ta-ha, o-ni-s-gi-ni ti-ga-do-v-si.

041308.cherokee: ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳᎯ ᏱᏙᏓᏍᏉᏑᎴᎵ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂ.  ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎬᏲᏑᎴᎥᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎥᏝ ᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᏳᏓᏑᏯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ.
041308.english: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
041308.phonetic: Qui-da hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; V-tla i-lv-hi-yu-hi yi-do-da-s-quo-su-le-li di-qua-la-si-de-ni.  Tsi-sa u-ne-tse-lv-gi; I-yu-no ni-gv-yo-su-le-v-na yi-gi v-tla tsa-tse-li-ga yu-da-su-ya a-qua-tse-li-ga ge-sv-i.

041309.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎥᏝ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂᏉ ᏧᏩᏒ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᏉᏰᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏍᎪᎵ.
041309.english: Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.
041309.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni Qui-da hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, v-tla di-qua-la-si-de-ni-quo tsu-wa-sv, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni di-quo-ye-ni a-le tsi-s-go-li.

041310.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎩᎶ ᎤᏙᏑᎵᏌᏛ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂᏉ ᏧᏩᏒ ᏧᏬᏑᎴᏗ ᎨᏐᎢ; ᏂᎬᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎨᏐᎢ.  ᎠᎴ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏂᏥᎥ ᏱᎩ.
041310.english: Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.
041310.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Gi-lo u-do-su-li-sa-dv tsi-ge-so-i tsu-la-si-de-ni-quo tsu-wa-sv tsu-wo-su-le-di ge-so-i; ni-gv-no u-da-nv-ga-lv-dv ge-so-i.  A-le ni-hi i-tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-dv, a-se-no v-tla ni-tsi-v yi-gi.

041311.cherokee: ᎠᎦᏔᎯᏳᏰᏃ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᎶᏄᎮᏍᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᏂᏥᎥ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᏱᎩ, ᎤᏛᏅᎩ.
041311.english: For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.
041311.phonetic: A-ga-ta-hi-yu-ye-no ge-sv-gi na-s-gi na u-lo-nu-he-s-gi; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di, V-tla ni-tsi-v i-tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-dv yi-gi, u-dv-nv-gi.

041312.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏬᏑᎴᎣᏁᎸ ᏧᎾᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎤᏄᏬᎥ; ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎢᎤᏪᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭᏍᎪ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏂᏨᏯᏛᏂᏏ?
041312.english: So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
041312.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-wo-su-le-o-ne-lv tsu-na-la-si-de-ni, a-le i-u-nu-wo-v; a-le ta-li-ne i-u-we-nv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsi-ga-ta-ha-s-go hi-a na-s-gi tsi-ni-tsv-ya-dv-ni-si?

041313.cherokee: ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏍᎩᏲᏎᎰᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏂᏥᏪᏍᎪᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᏍᏗ.
041313.english: Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.
041313.phonetic: Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, a-le Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, s-gi-yo-se-ho-i; a-le du-yu-go-dv ni-tsi-we-s-go-i, na-s-gi-ye-no nu-s-di.

041314.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏨᏰᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᏗᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏱᏗᏨᏲᏑᎴᎭ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏨᏒ ᎢᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏱᏗᏣᏓᏙᏑᎴᎭ.
041314.english: If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another's feet.
041314.phonetic: I-yu-no a-yv A-gi-gv-wi-yu-hi i-tsa-tse-li-ga a-le Di-tsv-ye-yo-hv-s-gi di-tsa-la-si-de-ni yi-di-tsv-yo-su-le-ha, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni i-tsv-sv i-tsa-la-si-de-ni yi-di-tsa-da-do-su-le-ha.

041315.english: For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.

041316.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎠᏥᎾᏝᎢ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᎾᏝᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏅᏓᏳᏅᏏᏛ.
041316.english: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
041316.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; A-tsi-na-tla-i v-tla u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu yi-ge-so-i, e-s-ga-quo u-na-tla-i; a-le v-tla a-tsi-nv-si-dv u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu yi-ge-so-i, e-s-ga-quo nv-da-yu-nv-si-dv.

041317.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏣᏓᏅᏔ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏂᏣᏛᏁᎭ.
041317.english: If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.
041317.phonetic: I-yu-no na-s-gi hi-a yi-tsi-ga-ta-ha, O-si-yu yi-tsa-da-nv-ta na-s-gi yi-ni-tsa-dv-ne-ha.

041318.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᏂᏥᎥ ᏱᏨᏯᏛᎦ; ᎦᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎦᏥᏯᏑᏰᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎪᏪᎸᎢ, "ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎣᏍᏓᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏌᎳᏓᏅ ᎦᏗᎨᏂ ᎠᏆᏡᏗᏍᎬᎢ."
041318.english: I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
041318.phonetic: V-tla ni-tsi-v yi-tsv-ya-dv-ga; ga-tsi-ga-ta-ha ga-tsi-ya-su-ye-dv ge-sv-i; a-se-no u-do-hi-yu-di-yi go-we-lv-i, "I-tsu-la-ha o-s-da-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gi u-sa-la-da-nv ga-di-ge-ni a-qua-tlu-di-s-gv-i."

041319.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏨᏃᏁᎭ, ᎠᏏᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎾ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏔᏅᎭ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
041319.english: Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.
041319.phonetic: Hna-quo i-tsv-no-ne-ha, a-si-quo na-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-nv-na, na-s-gi hna-quo ni-ga-li-s-ta-nv-ha i-tso-hi-yu-di-yi a-yv na-s-gi ge-sv-i.

041320.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎩᎶ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ ᏥᏅᏏᏛ, ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ, ᏅᏛᎩᏅᏏᏛ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎨᏍᏗ.
041320.english: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
041320.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; Gi-lo da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di tsi-nv-si-dv, a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di, gi-lo-no a-yv da-qua-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di, nv-dv-gi-nv-si-dv da-da-ni-lv-ge-s-di.

041321.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᏕᏯᏔᏁᎸ ᎤᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏩᏁᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᎭ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᏣᏛᏅ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎤᏓᏑᏯ ᏛᎩᎶᏄᎮᎵ.
041321.english: When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
041321.phonetic: Tsi-sa na-s-gi nu-we-sv u-de-ya-ta-ne-lv u-da-nv-dv-i, a-le gv-ni-ge-sv nu-wa-ne-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya u-do-hi-yu-hi-ha hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; hi-a tsi-ni-tsa-dv-nv a-si-yv-wi u-da-su-ya dv-gi-lo-nu-he-li.

041322.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᎾᏓᎧᎿᎭᏅᎩ ᎤᎾᏠᎬᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏛᎦ ᎤᏁᎵᏍᏗᏱ.
041322.english: Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.
041322.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi du-na-da-ka-hna-nv-gi u-na-tlo-gv-gi na-s-gi a-tsi-dv-ga u-ne-li-s-di-yi.

041323.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏴᏫᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏥᏌ ᎦᏁᏥᏱ ᎦᏍᏛᎦᎸᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎨᏳᎯ.
041323.english: Now there was leaning on Jesus' bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved.
041323.phonetic: A-si-yv-wi-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ge-sv Tsi-sa ga-ne-tsi-yi ga-s-dv-ga-lv-gi, na-s-gi Tsi-sa u-ge-yu-hi.

041324.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᏍᎫᏫᏎᎸᎩ ᎤᏛᏛᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎦᏛᎬᎢ.
041324.english: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.
041324.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no Qui-da na-s-gi u-li-s-gu-wi-se-lv-gi u-dv-dv-di-yi na-s-gi ge-sv-i ga-dv-gv-i.

041325.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎦᏁᏥᎢ ᎤᏍᏛᎦᎵ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᎪ?
041325.english: He then lying on Jesus' breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?
041325.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa ga-ne-tsi-i u-s-dv-ga-li hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ga-go?

041326.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ ᏥᏁᎸᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏊᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎤᏭᏅᏃ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ ᎤᏁᎸᎩ ᏧᏓᏏ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎤᏪᏥ.
041326.english: Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it. And when he had dipped the sop, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon.
041326.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv-gi; Na-s-gi ge-se-s-di a-gv-ha-lv-dv tsi-ne-lv-ha na-s-gi a-quu-nv-hi ge-se-s-di.  U-wu-nv-no a-gv-ha-lv-dv u-ne-lv-gi Tsu-da-si I-s-ga-la-di Sa-wa-ni u-we-tsi.

041327.cherokee: ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛᏃ ᎠᏥᏁᎸ ᏎᏓᏂ ᎤᏴᏎᎸᎩ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏄᎳᏉ ᎿᎭᏛᎦ.
041327.english: And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.
041327.phonetic: A-gv-ha-lv-dv-no a-tsi-ne-lv Se-da-ni u-yv-se-lv-gi.  Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Na-s-gi i-tsa-dv-ne-di ge-sv nu-la-quo hna-dv-ga.

041328.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏂᏅᏜᏓᎢ ᏳᏬᎵᏤ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎢ.
041328.english: Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
041328.phonetic: V-tla-no gi-lo a-ni-nv-dla-i yu-wo-li-tse nu-s-dv ga-dv-gv na-s-gi nu-we-se-lv-i.

041329.cherokee: ᏧᏓᏏᏰᏃ ᏕᎦᎶᏗ ᎦᏁᎲ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎢᎦᏛ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏌ ᏂᎦᏪᏏ ᎤᏁᎵᏒᎩ; ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏣᏩᎯ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᎬᏙᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏱᎩ; ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏘᎲᏏ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ.
041329.english: For some of them thought, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy those things that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.
041329.phonetic: Tsu-da-si-ye-no de-ga-lo-di ga-ne-hv nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi i-ga-dv, hi-a Tsi-sa ni-ga-we-si u-ne-li-sv-gi; Go-hu-s-di tsa-wa-hi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi ge-sv i-yu go-hu-s-di i-gv-do-di; a-le hi-a yi-gi; go-hu-s-di ti-hv-si u-yo i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi.

041330.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ ᎤᎩᏒ, ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎨᏒᎩ.
041330.english: He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.
041330.phonetic: Hna-quo-no na-s-gi, a-gv-ha-lv-dv u-gi-sv, gi-la-quo i-yv-dv u-nu-go-tsv-gi, a-le sv-no-yi ge-sv-gi.

041331.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏄᎪᏨ, ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎿᎭᏉ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏓ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏂᎬᏂᏌ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏓ.
041331.english: Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
041331.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-nu-go-tsv, Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Hna-quo yv-wi u-we-tsi a-tsi-lv-quo-da, na-s-gi-no ni-gv-ni-sa U-ne-la-nv-hi a-tsi-lv-quo-da.

041332.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏄᏩᏂᏌᏅ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏯᏥᎸᏉᏔᏅ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏅᏓᎬᏂᏌᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏳᎸᏉᏔᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏓᏳᎸᏉᏔᏂ.
041332.english: If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
041332.phonetic: I-yu-no na-s-gi yi-nu-wa-ni-sa-nv U-ne-la-nv-hi ya-tsi-lv-quo-ta-nv, U-ne-la-nv-hi u-wa-sv nv-da-gv-ni-sa-ni na-s-gi da-yu-lv-quo-ta-ni, a-le gi-la-quo i-yv-dv da-yu-lv-quo-ta-ni.

041333.cherokee: ᏗᏥᏲᎵ, ᎠᏏ ᏞᎦ ᎢᏨᏰᎳᏗᏙᎭ; ᏍᎩᏲᎮᏍᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏥᏂᎦᏥᏪᏎᎸᎩ, "ᏫᏥᎦᏛ ᎥᏝ ᏫᏴᎨᏥᎶᎯ," ᏥᎦᏥᏲᏎᎸᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ.
041333.english: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
041333.phonetic: Di-tsi-yo-li, a-si tle-ga i-tsv-ye-la-di-do-ha; s-gi-yo-he-s-di; a-le na-s-gi-ya A-ni-tsu-si tsi-ni-ga-tsi-we-se-lv-gi, "Wi-tsi-ga-dv v-tla wi-yv-ge-tsi-lo-hi," tsi-ga-tsi-yo-se-lv-gi, na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo hna-quo ni-hi ni-tsv-we-se-ha.

041334.cherokee: ᎢᏤ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᎥᏏ, ᏗᏣᏓᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢᏳ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᏣᏓᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
041334.english: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
041334.phonetic: I-tse di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di i-tsv-v-si, di-tsa-da-ge-yu-hi-yu i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi; Na-s-gi-ya i-tsv-ge-yu-i-yu nu-li-s-ta-nv-i, na-s-gi na-s-quo di-tsa-da-ge-yu-hi-yu i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

041335.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᏣᏓᎨᏳᎯᏳ ᏱᎩ.
041335.english: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
041335.phonetic: Na-s-gi na-ni-v u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di ge-se-s-di s-gi-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ge-sv-i, i-yu-no di-tsa-da-ge-yu-hi-yu yi-gi.

041336.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎭᏢ ᎭᎢ?  ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎩ; ᏫᏥᎦᏛ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏴᎨᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ; ᎣᏂᏍᎩᏂ ᏓᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏕᏏ.
041336.english: Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.
041336.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni Qui-da hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ha-tlv ha-i?  Tsi-sa u-ne-tse-lv-gi; Wi-tsi-ga-dv v-tla hna-quo yv-ge-s-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga; o-ni-s-gi-ni da-s-gi-s-da-wa-de-si.

041337.cherokee: ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎦᎬᏍᏓᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ?  ᏂᎯ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏔᏂ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏓᏥᏂ ᎬᏅᎢ.
041337.english: Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.
041337.phonetic: Qui-da hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ga-do-no v-tla ye-li hna-quo ga-gv-s-da-wa-dv-s-di yi-gi?  Ni-hi nv-da-ga-li-s-do-ta-ni e-la-di da-tsi-ni gv-nv-i.

041338.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎩ; ᏥᏌ ᎠᏴ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏔᏂ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏘᏂ ᎲᏅᎢ?  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏪᏎᎭ; ᏣᏔᎦᏨᏯᎢ ᎥᏝ ᏴᏛᏴᎳᏏ ᎬᏂ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏍᏆᏓᏱᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
041338.english: Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.
041338.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-ne-tse-lv-gi; Tsi-sa a-yv nv-da-ga-li-s-do-ta-ni e-la-di ti-ni hv-nv-i?  U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-gv-we-se-ha; Tsa-ta-ga-tsv-ya-i v-tla yv-dv-yv-la-si gv-ni tso-i i-s-qua-da-yi-lv-hi ge-se-s-di.

041401.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏙ ᏳᏕᏍᏔᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᎡᏦᎢᏳᎲᎦ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏴ ᏍᎩᏲᎢᏳᎲᎦ.
041401.english: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
041401.phonetic: Tle-s-di i-tsa-da-nv-do yu-de-s-ta-ne-he-s-di; e-tso-i-yu-hv-ga U-ne-la-nv-hi, a-le na-s-quo a-yv s-gi-yo-i-yu-hv-ga.

041402.cherokee: ᎡᏙᏓ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᎤᏣᏔ ᏓᏓᏁᎸ.  ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛᎾ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, ᏱᏨᏃᏁᎴᎢ.  ᎦᏛᏅᎢᏍᏔᏂ ᎢᏥᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
041402.english: In my Father's house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
041402.phonetic: E-do-da ga-ne-lv-i u-tsa-ta da-da-ne-lv.  I-yu-no na-s-gi nu-s-dv-na yi-ge-se-i, yi-tsv-no-ne-le-i.  Ga-dv-nv-i-s-ta-ni i-tsi-yv-s-di-yi.

041403.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᏯᏆᏛᏅᎢᏍᏔᏁᏅ ᎢᏥᏴᏍᏗᏱ, ᏔᎵᏁ ᏛᏥᎷᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏋᏒ ᎠᏉᎸ ᏙᏓᏨᏯᏓᏂᎸᏥ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎨᎥ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᎿᎭᎢᏣᏕᏗᏱ.
041403.english: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.
041403.phonetic: I-yu-no ya-qua-dv-nv-i-s-ta-ne-nv i-tsi-yv-s-di-yi, ta-li-ne dv-tsi-lu-tsi a-le a-quv-sv a-quo-lv do-da-tsv-ya-da-ni-lv-tsi, na-s-gi a-yv ge-v na-s-quo na-hna i-tsa-de-di-yi.

041404.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᏫᏥᎦᏛ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᎦᏅᏅ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ.
041404.english: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.
041404.phonetic: A-le a-yv wi-tsi-ga-dv i-tsi-ga-ta-ha, a-le wi-ga-nv-nv i-tsi-ga-ta-ha.

041405.cherokee: ᏓᎻ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎥᏝ ᏲᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏫᎦᏛᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏲᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏫᎦᏅᏅᎢ.
041405.english: Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?
041405.phonetic: Da-mi hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, v-tla yo-tsi-ga-ta-ha wi-ga-dv-i; ga-do-no yi-ga-li-s-do-da yo-tsi-ga-ta-ha wi-ga-nv-nv-i.

041406.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏃᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏳᎪᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏛ.  ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᏱᎦᎷᏤᎰᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎶᎯᏎᎸᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ.
041406.english: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
041406.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; A-yv na-s-gi nv-no-hi, a-le du-yu-go-dv-i, a-le gv-ni-dv.  V-tla gi-lo A-ga-yv-li-ge-i yi-ga-lu-tse-ho-i, gv-ni a-yv a-gi-lo-hi-se-lv-hi tsi-ge-so-i.

041407.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏴ ᏱᏍᎩᎦᏔᎮᎢ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎡᏙᏓ ᏰᏥᎦᏔᎮᎢ; ᎪᎯᏃ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎡᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏥᎪᏩᏛᎲ.
041407.english: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
041407.phonetic: I-yu-no a-yv yi-s-gi-ga-ta-he-i na-s-quo E-do-da ye-tsi-ga-ta-he-i; Go-hi-no i-yu-da-le-nv-dv e-tsi-ga-ta-ha a-le e-tsi-go-wa-dv-hv.

041408.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏍᎩᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏰᎵᎦ ᏓᏲᏥᏰᎸᏂ.
041408.english: Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
041408.phonetic: Qui-li-gi hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, s-gi-na-nu-go-wi-si A-ga-yv-li-ge-i, hna-quo-no ye-li-ga da-yo-tsi-ye-lv-ni.

041409.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᏌ ᏂᎪᎯᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᏁᏓ, ᎠᏏᏍᎪᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏍᎩᎦᏙᎥᏍᎦ, ᏈᎵᎩ; ᎩᎶ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎪᎲᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎤᎪᎲᎯ ᎨᏐ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎯᏪᎠ; ᏍᎩᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ?
041409.english: Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?
041409.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-sa ni-go-hi-gi i-tsu-la-ha i-ne-da, a-si-s-go-no v-tla yi-s-gi-ga-do-v-s-ga, Qui-li-gi; gi-lo a-yv a-gi-go-hv-hi tsi-ge-so-i, u-go-hv-hi ge-so A-ga-yv-li-ge-i; ga-do-no hi-a ni-hi-we-a; S-gi-na-nu-go-wi-si A-ga-yv-li-ge-i?

041410.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᏲᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ ᏥᏯᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᏯᎥᎢ?  ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏥᏨᏃᎮᎮᎰᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᏋᏒᏉ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏖᏛ ᏱᏥᏁᎪᎢ, ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢᏍᎩᏂ ᏣᎩᏯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎰᎢ.
