041941.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᎦᏛᏅ ᎠᏫᎡᏗᏱ ᎪᏢᏒᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏫᏒᏗᏱ ᎢᏤ ᏗᏓᏂᏐᏗᏱ ᎪᏢᏒᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏥᏂᏌᏅ ᎠᏏ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
041941.english: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.
041941.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-ga-dv-nv a-wi-e-di-yi go-tlv-sv-gi; na-hna-no a-wi-sv-di-yi i-tse di-da-ni-so-di-yi go-tlv-sv-gi gi-lo a-tsi-ni-sa-nv a-si ni-ge-sv-na.

041942.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎤᏂᏅᏅᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᏛᏅᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᏗᏓᏂᏐᏗᏱᏰᏃ ᎾᎥᏉ ᎨᏒᎩ.
041942.english: There laid they Jesus therefore because of the Jews' preparation day; for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.
041942.phonetic: Na-hna-no u-ni-nv-nv-gi Tsi-sa nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-ga a-dv-nv-i-s-do-di-yi i-ga ge-sv-i; di-da-ni-so-di-yi-ye-no na-v-quo ge-sv-gi.

042001.cherokee: ᎠᎴᏂᏍᎬᎢᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ ᏑᎾᎴᎢᏳ, ᎠᏏᏉ ᎤᎵᏏᎬᎩ, ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᏅᏯ ᎠᎲᏛ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ.
042001.english: The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.
042001.phonetic: a-le-ni-s-gv-i-no i-ga su-na-do-da-qua-s-di ge-sv-i, Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi su-na-le-i-yu, a-si-quo u-li-si-gv-gi, u-lu-tsv-gi a-tse-li-s-dv-i, a-le u-go-hv-gi nv-ya a-hv-dv ge-sv-gi a-tse-li-s-dv-i.

042002.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏍᏆᎸᏔᏅᎩ, ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏭᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏐᎢ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎨᏳᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏫᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᏂᏁᏒ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏫᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏲᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏭᏂᏅᏅᎢ.
042002.english: Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him.
042002.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-s-qua-lv-ta-nv-gi, Sa-wa-ni Qui-da wu-lu-tse-lv-gi, a-le na-s-quo so-i a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, na-s-gi tsi-sa u-ge-yu-hi, hi-a wi-ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; U-ni-ne-sv u-ni-nu-go-wi-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-tse-li-s-dv-i, a-le v-tla yo-tsi-ga-ta-ha-wu-ni-nv-nv-i.

042003.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏐᎢ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ.
042003.english: Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.
042003.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di Qui-da u-nu-go-tsv-gi, a-le so-i a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, a-le a-tse-li-s-dv u-ni-lu-tsv-gi.

042004.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏚᏂᏍᏆᎸᏔᏅᎩ; ᏐᎢᏃ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᏓᎩᏴᎩ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ.
042004.english: So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.
042004.phonetic: Hna-quo-no na-s-gi a-ni-ta-li i-tsu-la-ha du-ni-s-qua-lv-ta-nv-gi; so-i-no a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi du-da-gi-yv-gi, i-gv-yi a-tse-li-s-dv u-lu-tsv-gi.

042005.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏗᏌᏓᏛ ᏚᎪᎲᎩ ᏙᎴᏛ ᏗᏄᏬ ᏕᎦᏅᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏴᎴᎢ.
042005.english: And he stooping down, and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.
042005.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-di-sa-da-dv du-go-hv-gi do-le-dv di-nu-wo de-ga-nv-gi; a-se-no v-tla yu-yv-le-i.

042006.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᎣᏂᏉ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᏭᏴᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎪᎲᎩ ᏙᎴᏛ ᏗᏄᏬ ᏕᎦᏅᎢ.
042006.english: Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie,
042006.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Sa-wa-ni Qui-da o-ni-quo u-lu-tsv-gi, a-le a-tse-li-s-dv wu-yv-lv-gi, a-le du-go-hv-gi do-le-dv di-nu-wo de-ga-nv-i.

042007.cherokee: ᎠᏯᏠᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᏚᎶᏔᏅᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏙᎴᏛ ᏗᏄᏬ ᏕᎦᏅ ᏱᎦᏁᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎦᏇᏅᎯ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏫᎦᏅᎩ.
042007.english: And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.
042007.phonetic: A-ya-tlo-no u-ni-s-du-lo-ta-nv-hi v-tla do-le-dv di-nu-wo de-ga-nv yi-ga-ne-i, u-ne-la-gi ga-que-nv-hi i-yv-dv wi-ga-nv-gi.

042008.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏐᎢ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᎷᏨᎯ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ, ᎤᏴᎸᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ.
042008.english: Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.
042008.phonetic: Hna-quo-no na-s-quo so-i a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, i-gv-yi u-lu-tsv-hi a-tse-li-s-dv, u-yv-lv-gi; a-le u-go-hv-gi a-le u-wo-hi-yu-nv-gi.

042009.cherokee: ᏝᏰᏃ ᎠᏏ ᏯᏃᎵᎨ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎪᏪᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏧᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
042009.english: For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead.
042009.phonetic: Tla-ye-no a-si ya-no-li-ge ga-dv-gv go-we-lv-i, na-s-gi u-yo-hu-sv tsu-le-hi-so-di ge-sv-i.

042010.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏒᎩ, ᏔᎵᏁ ᏙᏧᏁᏅᏒ ᏭᏂᎶᏒᎩ.
042010.english: Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
042010.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-na-da-nv-sv-gi, ta-li-ne do-tsu-ne-nv-sv wu-ni-lo-sv-gi.

042011.cherokee: ᎺᎵᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏙᎬᎩ, ᏓᏠᏱᎲᎩ; ᏓᏠᏱᎲᎩ ᎤᏗᏌᏓᏛᎩ, ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎭᏫᏂ ᏫᏚᎧᎾᏅᎩ,
042011.english: But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down, and looked into the sepulchre,
042011.phonetic: Me-li-s-gi-ni a-tse-li-s-dv do-yi-di-tlv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi ga-do-gv-gi, da-tlo-yi-hv-gi; da-tlo-yi-hv-gi u-di-sa-da-dv-gi, a-tse-li-s-dv ha-wi-ni wi-du-ka-na-nv-gi,

042012.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏫᏚᎪᎲᎩ ᎤᏁᎬ ᏚᎾᏣᏅᏛᎩ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᏃᎸᎩ, ᏌᏉ ᎤᎵᏍᏘᏅᎢ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᏚᏜᏓᏌᏔᏅᎢ.
042012.english: And seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
042012.phonetic: A-le wi-du-go-hv-gi u-ne-gv du-na-tsa-nv-dv-gi a-ni-ta-li di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi du-no-lv-gi, sa-quo u-li-s-ti-nv-i, so-i-no du-dla-sa-ta-nv-i.

042013.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎨᏴ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᏙᎭᏠᏱᎭ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎤᏂᏁᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏅᏅ ᏂᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
042013.english: And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
042013.phonetic: Hi-a-no nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-ge-yv, ga-do-no do-ha-tlo-yi-ha?  Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha u-ni-ne-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-qua-tse-li-ga, a-le u-ni-nv-nv ni-tsi-ga-ta-hv-na ge-sv-i.

042014.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎦᏙᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏬᎵᏤ ᏥᏌ ᎨᏒᎢ.
042014.english: And when she had thus said, she turned herself back, and saw Jesus standing, and knew not that it was Jesus.
042014.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv u-ga-ta-hv-sv-gi; a-le u-go-hv-gi Tsi-sa ga-do-gv-gi, a-le v-tla yu-wo-li-tse Tsi-sa ge-sv-i.

042015.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᎨᏴ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᏙᎭᏠᏱᎭ?  ᎦᎪ ᎯᏲᎭ?  ᎠᏫᏒᏗᏱ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎤᏪᎵᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎯᏍᎦᏯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎯᎾᏫᏛᎮᏍᏗ, ᏍᎩᏃᎲᏏ ᏫᏅᏅᎢ, ᎠᏴᏃ ᏓᏥᎾᏫᏛᎯ.
042015.english: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
042015.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-ge-yv, ga-do-no do-ha-tlo-yi-ha?  ga-go hi-yo-ha?  A-wi-sv-di-yi o-s-dv i-yu-wa-hna-de-gi u-we-li-sv-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Hi-s-ga-ya, i-yu-no ni-hi hi-na-wi-dv-he-s-di, s-gi-no-hv-si wi-nv-nv-i, a-yv-no da-tsi-na-wi-dv-hi.

042016.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎺᎵ.  ᎺᎵ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎳᏉᏂ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏛᎬᎢ, ᏗᏍᏇᏲᎲᏍᎩ.
042016.english: Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, Master.
042016.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Me-li.  Me-li u-ga-ta-hv-sv hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; La-quo-ni, na-s-gi ga-dv-gv-i, Di-s-que-yo-hv-s-gi.

042017.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏍᏆᏒᏂᎸᎩ, ᏝᏰᏃ ᎠᏏ ᎡᏙᏙᏱ ᏫᏯᏆᎵᏌᎳᏓᏅ; ᎮᎾᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᏗᏂᏅᎢ, ᎯᎠᏃ ᏫᏂᎩᏪᏏ; ᎡᏙᏙᏱ ᎠᏆᎵᏌᎳᏓᏂᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏥᏙᏙᏱ; ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏱ.
042017.english: Jesus saith unto her, Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God.
042017.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tle-s-di s-qua-sv-ni-lv-gi, tla-ye-no a-si e-do-do-yi wi-ya-qua-li-sa-la-da-nv; he-na-quo-s-gi-ni o-tsa-li-nv-tli di-ni-nv-i, hi-a-no wi-ni-gi-we-si; E-do-do-yi a-qua-li-sa-la-da-ni-di a-le ni-hi I-tsi-do-do-yi; A-qua-tse-li-ga U-ne-la-nv-hi-yi, a-le i-tsa-tse-li-ga U-ne-la-nv-hi-yi.

042018.cherokee: ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᎵᏃᎮᏔᏅᎢ.
042018.english: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken these things unto her.
042018.phonetic: Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi u-lu-tsv-gi a-le du-no-ne-lv-gi a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-go-hv U-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le na-s-gi nu-we-sv u-li-no-he-ta-nv-i.

042019.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᎦ ᎤᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴᏂᏍᎬ ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏓᏍᏚᎲᎩ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎥᎢ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏓᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ, ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᏥᏌ, ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᎴᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎢᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
042019.english: Then the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, came Jesus and stood in the midst, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
042019.phonetic: Hna-quo-no na-hi-yu i-ga u-sv-i, a-le-ni-s-gv su-na-do-da-qua-s-di ge-sv-i, ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi da-s-du-hv-gi a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi da-ni-la-wi-v-i, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv da-ni-s-ga-i-hv A-ni-tsu-si, u-lu-tsv-gi Tsi-sa, a-ye-li u-le-nv-gi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv i-tsa-tse-li-ga ge-se-s-di.

042020.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᏚᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸᎩ ᏧᏬᏰᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏆᎨᏂ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏓᏛᎩ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
042020.english: And when he had so said, he shewed unto them his hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.
042020.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, du-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv-gi tsu-wo-ye-ni a-le a-s-qua-ge-ni.  Hna-quo-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi o-s-dv u-na-da-nv-da-dv-gi u-ni-go-hv U-gv-wi-yu-hi.

042021.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎢᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎡᏙᏓ ᎠᎩᏅᏒᎢ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏨᏅᎵ.
042021.english: Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
042021.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ta-li-ne Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv i-tsa-tse-li-ga ge-se-s-di.  E-do-da a-gi-nv-sv-i na-s-gi-ya i-tsv-nv-li.

042022.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᏚᏦᏔᎲᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏗᏣᏓᏂᎸᎩ.
042022.english: And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost:
042022.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, du-tso-ta-hv-gi, hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do di-tsa-da-ni-lv-gi.

042023.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᏕᏥᏲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏗᏲᏒᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᏕᏥᏂᏱᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᏂᏴᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
042023.english: Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.
042023.phonetic: Gi-lo u-s-ga-nv-tsv de-tsi-yo-s-ge-s-di di-yo-sv-hi ge-se-s-di, gi-lo-no u-s-ga-nv-tsv de-tsi-ni-yi-s-ge-s-di di-ga-ni-yv-di ge-se-s-di.

042024.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏓᎻ, ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎨᎳ, ᏗᏗᎹ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎨᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎷᏨ.
042024.english: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
042024.phonetic: A-se-no Da-mi, ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv ge-la, di-di-ma tsu-do-i-dv, v-tla yi-ge-le Tsi-sa u-lu-tsv.

042025.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏐᎢ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎣᏥᎪᎥ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᎪᎢᏳᎲᎦ ᎬᏂ ᏧᏬᏰᏂ ᏴᎩ ᏚᏰᎸᏒ ᏱᏓᎩᎪᎲ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏰᏌᏛ ᏯᏋᏔᏅ ᏯᎩᏃᏟᏍᏔᏅ ᏴᎩ ᏚᏰᎸᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏆᎨᏂ ᏯᏆᏐᎾᏛ.
042025.english: The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord. But he said unto them, Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe.
042025.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-ni-so-i a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; O-tsi-go-v U-gv-wi-yu-hi.  A-se-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; V-tla yv-ga-go-i-yu-hv-ga gv-ni tsu-wo-ye-ni yv-gi du-ye-lv-sv yi-da-gi-go-hv, a-le tsi-ye-sa-dv ya-quv-ta-nv ya-gi-no-tli-s-ta-nv yv-gi du-ye-lv-sv-i, a-le a-s-qua-ge-ni ya-qua-so-na-dv.

042026.cherokee: ᏧᏁᎵᏁᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᏂᏯᎥᎩ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎻ ᎨᎸᎩ.  ᏥᏌ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᏗᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏓᏍᏚᎲᎩ, ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᎴᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎢᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
042026.english: And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: then came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, Peace be unto you.
042026.phonetic: Tsu-ne-li-ne-no i-ga ta-li-ne gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ni-ya-v-gi ga-li-tso-de, a-le Da-mi ge-lv-gi.  Tsi-sa u-lu-tsv-gi, di-ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi da-s-du-hv-gi, a-ye-li u-le-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv i-tsa-tse-li-ga ge-se-s-di.

042027.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏓᎻ; ᎡᏍᎦ ᎡᏅᎦ ᎯᏰᏌᏛᎢ, ᏘᎪᎵᏯ ᏗᏉᏰᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏍᎦ ᎡᏅᎦ ᏦᏰᏂ ᏥᏍᏆᎨᏂ ᏩᏐᎾᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᎾ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
042027.english: Then saith he to Thomas, reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing.
042027.phonetic: Hna-quo-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Da-mi; E-s-ga e-nv-ga hi-ye-sa-dv-i, ti-go-li-ya di-quo-ye-ni, a-le e-s-ga e-nv-ga tso-ye-ni tsi-s-qua-ge-ni wa-so-na-da, a-le tle-s-di no-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-na yi-ge-se-s-di, ho-hi-yu-hv-s-gi-s-gi-ni ge-se-s-di.

042028.cherokee: ᏓᎻ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ!
042028.english: And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God.
042028.phonetic: Da-mi u-ne-tsv-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi a-qua-tse-li-ga, a-le Tsa-ne-la-nv-hi a-qua-tse-li-ga!

042029.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᏍᎩᎪᏩᏛ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ; ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎬᎩᎪᎲᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᎬᏉᎯᏳᎲᏍᎪᎢ.
042029.english: Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
042029.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-s-gi-go-wa-dv nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da ho-hi-yu-hv-s-ga; o-si-yu i-yu-na-li-s-da-ne-di gv-gi-go-hv-hi ni-ge-sv-na a-se-no tsi-gv-quo-hi-yu-hv-s-go-i.

042030.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏛᎩ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎠᏂ ᎪᏪᎵᎯ ᏂᎪᏪᎸᎾ;
042030.english: And many other signs truly did Jesus in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book:
042030.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-dv-gi u-tli i-ga-i u-s-qua-ni-go-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv Tsi-sa gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, a-ni go-we-li-hi ni-go-we-lv-na;

042031.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᎪᏪᎳ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᏦᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᏃ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏙᎥ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏰᎢᎸᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
042031.english: But these are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name.
042031.phonetic: Hi-a-s-gi-ni go-we-la i-tso-hi-yu-di-yi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-i, a-le na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi ge-sv-i, i-tso-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-no gv-ni-dv i-tsa-tse-li i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, na-s-gi du-do-v nv-da-yu-li-ye-i-lv-s-do-di-yi.

042101.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸ, ᏥᏌ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏛᏁᎸᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏔᏈᎵᏯ ᎥᏓᎸᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎩ.
042101.english: After these things Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he himself.
042101.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv, Tsi-sa ta-li-ne gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-dv-ne-lv-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi Ta-qui-li-ya v-da-lv-i.  Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv-gi gv-ni-ge-sv nu-dv-ne-lv-gi.

042102.cherokee: ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᏂᏅᎩ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎻ ᏗᏗᎹ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏁᏓᏂᎵ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎡᎯ ᎨᏂ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏤᏈᏗ ᏧᏪᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ.
042102.english: There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.
042102.phonetic: I-tsu-la-ha a-ni-nv-gi Sa-wa-ni Qui-da, a-le Da-mi Di-di-ma tsu-do-i-dv, a-le Ne-da-ni-li Ge-li-li e-hi Ge-ni ga-du-hv-i, a-le Tse-qui-di tsu-we-tsi, a-le a-ni-ta-li nv-wa-na-da-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi.

042103.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᎦᏯᎷᏂ.  ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏓᏕᏏ.  ᎤᏁᏅᏒᎩ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏣᏅᎩ; ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏳᏂᏂᏴᎮᎢ.
042103.english: Simon Peter saith unto them, I go a fishing. They say unto him, We also go with thee. They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing.
042103.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni Qui-da hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-ga-ya-lu-ni.  Hi-a nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; A-yv na-s-quo da-de-si.  U-ne-nv-sv-gi, gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv tsi-yu-hi u-na-tsa-nv-gi; na-hi-yu-no sv-no-yi v-tla go-hu-s-di yu-ni-ni-yv-he-i.

042104.cherokee: ᏑᎾᎴᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᏥᏌ ᎤᏩᏝᎥ ᎦᏙᎬᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏂᎦᏔᎮ ᏥᏌ ᎨᏒᎢ.
042104.english: But when the morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.
042104.phonetic: Su-na-le-no nu-li-s-ta-nv, Tsi-sa u-wa-tla-v ga-do-gv-gi; a-se-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi v-tla ya-ni-ga-ta-he Tsi-sa ge-sv-i.

042105.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏗᏥᏲᎵ, ᎢᏥᏂᏌᎠᏧ?  ᎥᏝ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
042105.english: Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No.
042105.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Di-tsi-yo-li, i-tsi-ni-sa-a-tsu?  v-tla, u-na-dv-nv-gi.

042106.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏥᏳᎯ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ ᎢᏥᎦᏯᎷᎥᎦ, ᏙᏓᏥᏩᏛᎯᏃ.  ᎰᏩᏃ ᎤᏂᎦᏯᎷᏅᎩ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎾᏎᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏛ ᎠᏣᏗ.
042106.english: And he said unto them, Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.
042106.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Tsi-yu-hi a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv i-tsi-ga-ya-lu-v-ga, do-da-tsi-wa-dv-hi-no.  Ho-wa-no u-ni-ga-ya-lu-nv-gi, hna-quo-no v-tla ye-li gv-wa-na-se-hi-s-di yi-ge-se-i, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi u-ni-tsa-dv a-tsa-di.

042107.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎨᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏈᏓ; ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎦᏚᎢ ᎤᏄᏬᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏠᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏂᏚᏄᏩᎥᎾᏰᏃ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏓᎵ ᏭᎵᏔᏗᏅᏒᎩ.
042107.english: Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord. Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he girt his fisher's coat unto him, (for he was naked,) and did cast himself into the sea.
042107.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi Tsi-sa u-ge-yu-hi hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Qui-da; U-gv-wi-yu-hi na-s-gi.  Hna-quo-no Sa-wa-ni Qui-da u-dv-ga-nv na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i, ga-du-i u-nu-wo-s-di u-da-tlo-s-ta-nv-gi, ni-du-nu-wa-v-na-ye-no ge-sv-gi, a-le v-da-li wu-li-ta-di-nv-sv-gi.

042108.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏃ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏥᏳ ᎤᎾᏦᏛᎩ, ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎢᏅᎯᏳ ᏱᎨᏎ ᏙᏱ, ᏔᎵᏧᏈᏉ ᎢᏯᎵᎩᏳᏍᏈᏛ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏅᏩᏍᏛᎩ, ᎠᎦᏯᎷᏗ ᎠᏣᏗ ᎠᎧᎵᏬᎯ ᎠᏂᎾᏏᏁᎲᎩ.
042108.english: And the other disciples came in a little ship; (for they were not far from land, but as it were two hundred cubits,) dragging the net with fishes.
042108.phonetic: A-ni-so-i-no a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi tsi-yu u-na-tso-dv-gi, u-ni-lu-tsv-gi, v-tla-ye-no i-nv-hi-yu yi-ge-se do-yi, ta-li-tsu-qui-quo i-ya-li-gi-yu-s-qui-dv i-yv-dv nv-wa-s-dv-gi, a-ga-ya-lu-di a-tsa-di a-ka-li-wo-hi a-ni-na-si-ne-hv-gi.

042109.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏚᎩᏒᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎩ ᎠᏥᎸᎭ ᎪᏛᎩ, ᎠᏣᏗᏃ ᎥᎬᏓᎥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏚ ᏓᎲᎩ.
042109.english: As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
042109.phonetic: U-na-du-gi-sv-no u-ni-go-hv-gi a-tsi-lv-ha go-dv-gi, a-tsa-di-no v-gv-da-v-gi, a-le ga-du da-hv-gi.

042110.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎡᏗᏥᏄᎦ ᎢᎦᏛ ᎠᏣᏗ ᎩᎳᏉ ᏥᏕᏥᏂᏴ.
042110.english: Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.
042110.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; E-di-tsi-nu-ga i-ga-dv a-tsa-di gi-la-quo tsi-de-tsi-ni-yv.

042111.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏭᏣᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏱ ᏭᎾᏏᏃᎸᎩ ᎠᎦᏯᎷᏗ ᎠᎧᎵᏬᎯ ᏣᏁᏆ ᎠᏣᏗ, ᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎯᏍᎩᏍᎪᎯ ᏦᎢᎦᎵ ᎤᏂᎥᎩ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏒᎦᏃ ᎾᏂᎥᎽ ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᎦᏯᎷᏗ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏲᏤᎢ.
042111.english: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land full of great fishes, and hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken.
042111.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni Qui-da wu-tsa-nv-gi, a-le do-yi wu-na-si-no-lv-gi a-ga-ya-lu-di a-ka-li-wo-hi tsa-ne-qua a-tsa-di, s-go-hi-tsu-qui hi-s-gi-s-go-hi tso-i-ga-li u-ni-v-gi.  Na-s-gi-sv-ga-no na-ni-v-mu a-se-no a-ga-ya-lu-di v-tla yu-yo-tse-i.

042112.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎡᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᏄᎦ.  ᎥᏝᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᎾᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎦᎪ ᏂᎯ, ᎬᏬᏎᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎩᏰᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
042112.english: Jesus saith unto them, Come and dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
042112.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; E-tsa-li-s-da-yv-nu-ga.  V-tla-no gi-lo a-na-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, Ga-go ni-hi, gv-wo-se-di yi-ge-se-i, a-ni-ga-ta-hv-gi-ye-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i.

042113.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᎤᎩᏒᎩ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏁᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏚᏅᏁᎸᎩ.
042113.english: Jesus then cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.
042113.phonetic: Tsi-sa u-lu-tsv-gi u-gi-sv-gi ga-du a-le du-ne-lv-gi, a-le na-s-quo a-tsa-di du-nv-ne-lv-gi.

042114.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏦᎢᏁ ᏥᏌ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏛᏁᎸ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏚᎴᏅ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ.
042114.english: This is now the third time that Jesus shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead.
042114.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a hna-quo tso-i-ne Tsi-sa gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-dv-ne-lv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi u-yo-hu-sv du-le-nv nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-dv.

042115.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏃᏅ, ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ; ᏌᏩᏂ, ᏦᎾ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏂᎯ ᎤᏟ ᏂᏍᎩᎨᏳᎭ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎯᎠ?  ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᎥ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎯᎦᏔᎭ ᎬᎨᏳᎢᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏕᎮᎶᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᎠᏂᎩᎾ.
042115.english: So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.
042115.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-na-li-s-da-yv-no-nv, Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Sa-wa-ni Qui-da; Sa-wa-ni, Tso-na u-we-tsi, ni-hi u-tli ni-s-gi-ge-yu-ha e-s-ga-quo hi-a?  Hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; V-v, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, hi-ga-ta-ha gv-ge-yu-i-yu ge-sv-i.  Hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; De-he-lo-he-s-di di-qua-tse-li-ga u-ni-no-de-na a-ni-gi-na.

042116.cherokee: ᏔᎵᎾ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎤᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏌᏩᏂ, ᏦᎾ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏍᎩᎨᏳᎭᏧ?  ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᎥ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎯᎦᏔᎭ ᎬᎨᏳᎢᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏕᎮᎶᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ.
042116.english: He saith to him again the second time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
042116.phonetic: Ta-li-na hi-a nv-u-we-se-lv-gi; Sa-wa-ni, Tso-na u-we-tsi, s-gi-ge-yu-ha-tsu?  Hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; V-v Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, hi-ga-ta-ha gv-ge-yu-i-yu ge-sv-i.  Hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; De-he-lo-he-s-di di-qua-tse-li-ga u-ni-no-de-na.

042117.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏌᏩᏂ, ᏦᎾ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᏍᎩᎨᏳᎭᏧ?  ᏈᏓ ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎤᏓᏅᏓᏛᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᏦᎢᏁ ᏍᎩᎨᏳᎭᏧ ᎤᏬᏎᎸᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎯᎦᏔᎭ, ᎯᎦᏔᎭ ᎬᎨᏳᎢᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏕᎮᎶᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ.
042117.english: He saith unto him the third time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, Feed my sheep.
042117.phonetic: Tso-i-ne hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Sa-wa-ni, Tso-na u-we-tsi, s-gi-ge-yu-ha-tsu?  Qui-da u-yo-i-yu u-da-nv-da-dv-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi tso-i-ne s-gi-ge-yu-ha-tsu u-wo-se-lv-i.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di hi-ga-ta-ha, hi-ga-ta-ha gv-ge-yu-i-yu ge-sv-i.  Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; De-he-lo-he-s-di di-qua-tse-li-ga u-ni-no-de-na.

042118.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏪᏎᎭ; ᎯᏫᏅ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ ᏨᏒ ᎭᏓᏠᎯᎲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎮᏙᎲᎩ; ᏣᏛᏐᏅᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏙᏔᏐᎸᏔᏂ, ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏃ ᏓᏣᏠᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒ ᏮᏓᏣᏘᏅᏍᏔᏂ.
042118.english: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.
042118.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya, u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya hi-a ni-gv-we-se-ha; Hi-wi-nv tsi-ge-sv-gi tsv-sv ha-da-tlo-hi-hv-gi, a-le tsa-du-li-s-gv he-do-hv-gi; tsa-dv-so-nv-hi-s-gi-ni ge-se-s-di do-ta-so-lv-ta-ni, nv-wa-da-le-no da-tsa-tlo-li, a-le ni-tsa-du-li-s-gv-na ge-sv wv-da-tsa-ti-nv-s-ta-ni.

042119.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ ᎤᏪᏛᏨᎩ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎸᏉᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏁᏪᏒ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ.
042119.english: This spake he, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, Follow me.
042119.phonetic: Na-s-gi nu-we-sv-gi u-we-dv-tsv-gi i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di-yi u-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi-no u-lv-quo-do-di-yi U-ne-la-nv-hi.  Na-s-gi-no ne-we-sv, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; S-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga.

042120.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏒᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎨᏳᎯ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏛᎦᎸᏅᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᏁᏥᏱ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᏥᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏡᏗᏍᎩ.
042120.english: Then Peter, turning about, seeth the disciple whom Jesus loved following; which also leaned on his breast at supper, and said, Lord, which is he that betrayeth thee?
042120.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-ga-ta-hv-sv u-go-hv-gi a-da-s-da-wa-di-sv-gi Tsi-sa u-ge-yu-hi a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, na-s-gi u-s-dv-ga-lv-nv-hi Tsi-sa ga-ne-tsi-yi a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-i, a-le hi-a i-yu-we-dv tsi-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ga-go na-s-gi tsa-tlu-di-s-gi.

042121.cherokee: ᏈᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎪᎲ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏥᏌ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎯᎠᎾᏃ ᎦᏙᎢ?
042121.english: Peter seeing him saith to Jesus, Lord, and what shall this man do?
042121.phonetic: Qui-da na-s-gi u-go-hv, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Tsi-sa; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, hi-a-na-no ga-do-i?

042122.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏥᎷᏨᎭ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ ᎡᎮᏍᏗ ᏯᏇᎵᏒ, ᎦᏙ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏨᏙᏗ?  ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦᏉ.
042122.english: Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me.
042122.phonetic: Tsi-sa hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; I-yu-no tsi-lu-tsv-ha gv-di-s-gi e-he-s-di ya-que-li-sv, ga-do na-s-gi tsv-do-di?  S-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga-quo.

042123.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᏁᎲ ᎤᏰᎵᏒᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏛᏲᎱᏏ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎥᏝ ᏴᏛᏲᎱᏏ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏬᏎᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏥᎷᏨᎭ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ ᎡᎮᏍᏗ ᏯᏇᎵᏒ, ᎦᏙ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏨᏙᏗ.
042123.english: Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die: yet Jesus said not unto him, He shall not die; but, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee?
042123.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-na-da-nv-tli a-ne-hv u-ye-li-sv-gi hi-a na-ni-we-s-gv-i, na-s-gi a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi v-tla a-dv-yo-hu-si.  A-se-no Tsi-sa v-tla yv-dv-yo-hu-si v-tla yu-wo-se-le-i, hi-a-quo-s-gi-ni nu-we-se-lv-gi; I-yu-no tsi-lu-tsv-ha gv-di-s-gi e-he-s-di ya-que-li-sv, ga-do na-s-gi tsv-do-di.

042124.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ Ꮎ ᎠᏓᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏥᎧᏃᎮᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏬᏪᎳᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏗᎦᏔᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
042124.english: This is the disciple which testifieth of these things, and wrote these things: and we know that his testimony is true.
042124.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a na a-da-s-da-wa-di-do-hi hi-a tsi-ka-no-he-ha, a-le na-s-gi hi-a u-wo-we-la-nv-hi tsi-gi, a-le i-di-ga-ta-ha na-s-gi ka-no-he-s-gv u-do-hi-yu-hi-ya ge-sv-i.

042125.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏏ ᎤᏣᏔ ᏥᏌ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏱᎪᏪᏔᏅ, ᎦᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏯᎧᎵᎩᏉ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎪᏪᎳᏅᎯ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
042125.english: And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written. Amen.
042125.phonetic: A-le a-si u-tsa-ta Tsi-sa tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-hi, na-s-gi i-yu-no ni-ga-dv yi-go-we-ta-nv, ga-da-nv-te-s-gv e-lo-hi na-s-quo ya-ka-li-gi-quo go-we-li na-s-gi di-go-we-la-nv-hi.  E-me-nv.
